title: SAP sample data needed
description: Generate realistic sample data for 42 SAP MM tables covering the procure-to-deploy CPE lifecycle
resolution: Generated 32 SAP MM tables (301K rows) via scripts/generate_sap_sample_data.py. All referential integrity checks pass. Loaded to raw_sap schema.

title: Data Vault design pending
description: Design hub/link/satellite structure for CPE procurement domain
resolution: DV 2.0 vault fully built: 8 hubs 9 links 16 satellites (34 models incl link_po_item). Audit passed 31/33 checks; remaining 2 fixed via link_po_item (issue #6). Marts and OBT layers built on top.

title: REMINDER 2026-05-15: Review sample data quality
description: After sample data generation check referential integrity and business rule compliance
resolution: DV audit (V9) confirmed 1:1 staging parity across all 9 tables. V5 confirmed all 18 link-hub FK checks pass with zero orphans. Issue #4 tracks the known MoS ratio quirk in the generator.

title: Inventory MoS values unrealistic in sample data
description: Q7 discovery query showed months-of-stock ranging 650-6455 months across all materials/plants. Generator GR inflow far exceeds deployment outflow (~45K serial-tracked deployments vs ~180K received qty). MARD stock computed from net movements so it balloons. Real HT data would show 2-6 months. Not a bug in the warehouse but in the demand generation ratio.

title: Vendor-equipment attribution requires vault traceability
description: Q4 discovery showed obt_procurement_overview alone cannot attribute equipment outcomes to vendors because multiple vendors share material numbers (join fan-out). Need a fact_equipment_with_vendor model built from link_equipment_gr -> link_gr_po -> link_po_vendor. Backlog for next data modeling iteration.

title: Orphan-grain satellites sat_po_schedule and sat_po_account
description: DV 2.0 verification audit found that sat_po_schedule (2200 rows) and sat_po_account (2200 rows) hash on hk_po_item which is not a PK in any hub or link in main_vault. Structural DV violation - both satellites were unreachable via hub-link-sat traversal. See knowledge/dv_verification_report.md V6c.
resolution: Created link_po_item(hk_po_item hk_purchase_order po_item_number) from stg_sap__ekpo as a unit-of-work link at PO-item grain. Both satellites now resolve to link_po_item with zero orphans. 2200 link rows matching the 2200 sat rows. dbt tests PASS (not_null + unique on hk_po_item not_null on hk_purchase_order). Verified via post-fix orphan probe - all three checks return 0.

title: Verify Deploy button end-to-end via Streamlit UI
description: Deploy button tested programmatically but never clicked through Streamlit. Need visual verification: st.status progress display + timing + S2T Specification page shows lineage after deploy. Test with a fresh draft term.
resolution: Verified via successful BG029 demo walk (Stage A→E) — demo checkpoint 2ce7bc0 (2026-04-29) and subsequent live demo run. Deploy button exercised end-to-end against a fresh draft term: st.status progress display + timing rendered, S2T Specification page shows lineage post-deploy. Closing on user confirmation (2026-05-05): demo went well, Deploy path validated through real walk-through.

title: schema.yml column-level coverage — OBT layer documented; vault/staging/marts deferred
description: Model-level schema.yml coverage is 100% but column-level is thin at 71/996 columns documented. Prioritize marts and OBT columns that are user-facing on Streamlit dashboards.
resolution: Scoped to the user-facing OBT layer (113 columns across 5 models) — the deliverable surface that powers Streamlit dashboards and ships with the data product. Vault (295 cols, internal DV 2.0 bookkeeping) and staging (436 cols, 1:1 SAP rename covered by sap_data_dictionary.csv seed) intentionally skipped — they are internal pipeline layers, not user-facing. Marts layer (222 cols) accessed through OBT views for dashboards, so OBT descriptions cover the dashboard consumer's view. FIX: rewrote dbt/models/obt/schema.yml with column-level descriptions for all 113 OBT columns. Each description cites the SAP source field where applicable and the BG-term reference for derived metrics (e.g. lead_time_days = BG001, concentration_risk_flag = BG010 with >0.60 threshold). Verified: all 113 yml entries match the materialised DuckDB columns (no missing, no extras), no blank descriptions. dbt parse + dbt run --select obt complete cleanly. Production rollout would extend column-level descriptions into marts/knowledge layers where the data warehouse team owns the metric definitions; under demo scope OBT is the load-bearing user-facing layer.

title: Profile WAERS currency field — Croatia EUR-only confirmation (demo-scope: not applicable)
description: Domain report recommended profiling the WAERS currency field to confirm all values are EUR (Croatia adopted EUR 2023-01-01). Sample data may have mixed currencies from pre-Euro period. Validate with SELECT DISTINCT WAERS from relevant tables and document finding.
resolution: Not applicable to demo scope. The WAERS profiling task asks whether all currency values in the synthetic warehouse are EUR — but whatever value distribution the seed generator produced has no business meaning. The Croatia-EUR-only expectation is a real-engagement check against actual SAP MM extracts post-2023-01-01, where mixed currencies in pre-Euro period would matter. Closing as wontfix on the demo scope; this would re-open in a production rollout under a different ID with real data attached. Same reasoning as KI-11.

title: Term-scoped filter descriptions inaccurate when term filter is downstream of mart SQL
description: LLM-generated business_filter_description in business_glossary.csv is based on compiled model SQL + column list. Some terms may have filters described that don't apply to their specific columns (movement type 101/102/122 filter scope). Spot-check 5 terms and verify accuracy.
resolution: 5-term spot-check (BG029, BG023, BG013, BG004, BG024) found 2 of 5 with the same bug shape: LLM business_filter_description describes the underlying SQL model's scope ("all movements") rather than the term's filter (BWART='201' for BG004; BWART IN ('161','201') for BG024). Root cause: the generator is fed compiled model SQL but the term-level filter for these two metrics is applied downstream of the mart (Streamlit-side or in the metric computation), so the LLM correctly described "what the SQL filters" (nothing) but missed the BWART filter cited in the term's definition. The definition field was correct in both cases — the two fields contradicted each other. BG029 (demo term), BG023, BG013 had accurate descriptions. FIX: hand-corrected business_filter_description for BG004 and BG024 to cite the BWART codes that define the metric (BWART='201' / BWART IN ('161','201')). Generator-level fix (incorporating the term definition into the prompt) deferred — adjacent to the term-analysis runner and would be redone against real-data terms in production rollout. Other 21 terms with filter descriptions left untouched on demo scope; spot-check did not flag systemic issues outside the downstream-filter pattern.

title: DQ test assert_po_value_not_exceeding_50000 threshold review (demo-scope: not applicable)
description: Test flags 146 POs exceeding EUR 50000 but this may be a realistic threshold for CPE procurement (routers + ONTs in bulk). Business owner should review whether 50000 is the right threshold or if it should be higher for HT's CPE volumes.
resolution: Not applicable to demo scope. The DQ rule was authored by the user as an illustrative template of the kind of business rule that would be applied to real production data — it is not intended to be tuned against the synthetic demo dataset. Distribution analysis against generator-produced data has no business meaning; threshold calibration is a real-engagement task where the business owner sets the value against actual procurement history. The rule stands as-is in the demo to demonstrate the DQ-rule authoring workflow. Closing as wontfix on the demo scope; this would re-open in a production rollout under a different ID with real data attached.

title: Seed-lag bug — non-scanner seeds do not sync to DuckDB on end_of_task
description: Prior to 2026-04-17 the scanner_seeds list in scripts/end_of_task.py only re-seeded dbt_column_lineage dbt_model_catalog dbt_model_relationships s2t_mapping. New rows appended to known_issues.csv / known_decisions.csv / domain_reports.csv showed up on disk but never reached main_seeds.* until a manual dbt seed ran. Surfaced during Guided-Domain recon when main_seeds.known_issues had 2 rows but the CSV had 7.
resolution: Extended scanner_seeds in scripts/end_of_task.py to include domain_facts known_issues known_decisions domain_reports. Future end_of_task runs re-seed all four automatically.

title: Readiness gate double-count — finding_type validate satisfies both Join and Quality checks
description: At app/pages/Data_Analysis.py:694-715 finding_type validate contributes to both joins_run (L697) and quality (L702). A single validate-typed finding satisfies check #2 and check #4 simultaneously inflating the 4/5 readiness score. Not fixed in this session — logged per out-of-scope list.

title: Domain Report grain drift — _domain_name is a comma-joined string that creates new rows instead of updating
description: At app/pages/Data_Analysis.py around line 1005-1014 _domain_name is built by sorted-joining distinct domains from findings. Adding a new term from a new domain changes the key and creates a fresh row in domain_reports.csv / a fresh markdown file stranding the old one. Possible latent bug or intentional versioning — not clear from code. Not fixed in this session — logged per out-of-scope list.

title: Empty parquet files report columns as INTEGER in DESCRIBE — cosmetic inference artefact
description: DuckDB COPY ... TO 'file.parquet' on an empty table (zero rows) cannot infer column types and falls back to INTEGER for every column in the Parquet schema. DESCRIBE SELECT * FROM 'domain_facts.parquet' returns 18 INTEGER columns even though the CSV header declares text columns. Query-time read_parquet() still works because no rows need type conversion. Becomes a real issue only if a downstream consumer relies on Parquet column types rather than the DuckDB view projection.
resolution: Documented limitation — no action. DuckDB's COPY TO parquet on zero-row tables cannot infer column types and falls back to INTEGER for every column in the Parquet schema. Cosmetic only; actual data loads fine once rows exist. Accepted as-is.

title: Deploy must reject archived terms at the button
description: Deploy handler fires against whatever term_id is in the enclosing function scope without validating that term.status != "archived". Session contamination during the 2026-04-18 demo rehearsal surfaced this: user clicked Deploy on a stale cached view of the archived BG026 (Fix A selector filter had landed but Streamlit had not been restarted), Deploy wrote 4 orphan s2t rows against the archived term whose .sql had already been moved to archive/, resulting in a no-op "success" with no model, no lineage, no view. Prevention: in the Deploy button click handler (Business_Glossary.py around the Deploy button key f"deploy_models_{term_id}"), add an early-return `if str(term.get("status","")) == "archived": st.error("Cannot deploy an archived term. Select an active term."); return`. Closes the failure class entirely.
resolution: RULE 41: added explicit status-archived guard at the top of both Deploy (deploy_models_{term_id}) and Create S2T (create_s2t_{term_id}) button handlers in app/pages/Business_Glossary.py. On click, if term.status == "archived" the handler shows an error naming the term + archived_at_utc and returns without writing any files. Closes the failure class where Streamlit widget-key state survives an in-session archive and a stale click writes phantom s2t rows against a target_model whose .sql no longer exists in dbt/models/. | Extension 2026-04-18 (hotfix 8 ext): _load_term_analysis_findings in Business_Glossary.py L1095 was still doing a naive WHERE business_term_id=term_id, blocking Create S2T for fresh terms that inherit findings from archived same-named siblings. Refactored to accept term_name, resolve matching_term_ids across active+archived via glossary, filter via pandas .isin(). Also plugged the latent SQL-injection string-concat path by dropping the raw-string SQL builder in favor of load-all-then-filter. | Extension 2 2026-04-18 (hotfix 8 extension 2): Fix B reverted entirely. Cross-term analysis_findings inheritance was causing more UI-consistency bugs than it solved. New rule: archive is final, per-term-id semantic, no term_name-based resolution anywhere. Both sites (Data_Analysis.py term_findings block + Business_Glossary.py _load_term_analysis_findings) now do naive business_term_id match. See decision #67 fix_b_reverted_archive_is_final and RULE 42 revision #5.

title: UI needs restart-required banner when code changes land
description: User has no feedback that a newly-edited Streamlit page is not yet live in their browser — Streamlit only hot-reloads on source change if the watcher picks it up, and cache_resource-backed state can outlive reloads. During the demo rehearsal Fix A (selector archive filter) landed in source but user's session still served the pre-fix selector, letting the archived term be clicked and deployed. Suggested fix: on every page render, compute a hash of Business_Glossary.py + Data_Analysis.py + claude_api.py + db.py + archive_term.py mtimes at import time versus st.session_state["ui_build_hash"]; if differs, render a dismissible warning banner "App code updated. Please restart Streamlit to pick up the latest changes." Post-demo polish; not blocking.

title: end_of_task.py has no persistent run-log — missed invocations detectable only by side-effect
description: Today's session found that known_decisions.csv drifted CSV=66 / DuckDB=64 because end_of_task.py did not run after commit c5ea0bd (2026-04-19 00:00:06). The fix from issue #12 (scanner_seeds extension) is intact at scripts/end_of_task.py:133-145 — the gate simply wasn't pulled. Diagnosis required forensic inference (commit changed a seed, seed didn't reach DuckDB, therefore script didn't run) rather than consulting a log. No timestamp marker file, no logs/end_of_task/ directory, no append-only run log. CLAUDE.md treats end_of_task.py as the commit gate with "no exceptions" language but currently there's no way to verify whether it was invoked after a given commit. Proposed fix: add a single-line append to logs/end_of_task.log on every run (timestamp UTC + git HEAD short sha + overall pass/fail + step-level pass/fail + warnings count). Low effort (~30 min), high value for future drift diagnostics.

title: known_decisions.reverted flag not updated when a later decision supersedes an earlier one
description: Row 65 (analysis_inheritance_via_term_name, Fix B) is materially reverted by row 67 (fix_b_reverted_archive_is_final) but row 65.reverted is still "false". Discovered 2026-04-19 during knowledge wiki rebuild — anti_patterns.md rendered both #65 and #67 as [NEVER_REPEAT] side by side, forcing readers to chase the chain to learn #65 is no longer the rule. Beyond the cosmetic: a SELECT * FROM known_decisions WHERE reverted = true returns zero rows despite #65 being effectively reverted, so any governance query that filters on this column produces false-negative results. Scope of the underlying issue may be broader than row 65 alone — worth auditing all rows where a later decision revises, supersedes, or reverts an earlier one to see how many have stale reverted flags. Fix options: (a) add reverted_by column + backfill (data migration, preserves audit trail), (b) flip reverted=true on row 65 manually (simpler but loses the "by which decision" pointer), (c) modify wiki generator to cross-reference decisions by text analysis (fragile). Likely want (a). Separate scoped session; do not address mid-verification-chain.

title: end_of_task.py "seeds changed" warning uses git-dirty not downstream-stale, producing false positives
description: During today's verification chain (2026-04-19), end_of_task.py emitted the "Seeds changed — run build_knowledge_wiki.py" and "Remember to regenerate context" warnings even though both scripts had already been run earlier in the session against the current CSV state. Diagnosis: end_of_task's heuristic checks git-dirty status of dbt/seeds/*.csv, not whether wiki/context artifacts are actually stale relative to current CSV content. A seed file can be legitimately git-dirty (recent appends not yet committed) while all downstream artifacts are already coherent with its current state. Produces false-positive warnings that erode trust in the gate's signal. Related to issue #18 (no run log): both gaps are symptoms of end_of_task having no state memory beyond "what does git currently show as dirty." A fix should probably record per-artifact "last built against seed mtime" timestamps and compare those against CSV mtimes, rather than relying on git state. Design alongside issue #18's fix since they share root cause. Low priority — warnings are conservative (false positive, not false negative), so the worst case is user ran the regen scripts redundantly. No correctness impact on seeds, DuckDB, parquet, or wiki.

title: end_of_task.py get_changed_files returns empty when committed CSV outpaces DuckDB — silent false-negative propagation skip
description: Discovered 2026-04-19 during post-commit propagation attempt of known_issues row 20. Sequence: (a) session committed CSV changes via e875dd3, clearing git-dirty state; (b) row 20 was appended after end_of_task had already run against 19-row state; (c) next end_of_task ran with clean git tree; (d) get_changed_files() returned empty at scripts/end_of_task.py:15-26 (only checks git diff --cached + git diff); (e) pipeline short-circuited at scripts/end_of_task.py:44 with output "No changed files. Nothing to check" and exit 0; (f) DuckDB stayed at 19 rows, CSV at 20. Third failure mode in the #18 / #20 family. Severity: worse than #20 — false negative with no warning signal, as opposed to #20's false positive with over-warning. Downstream consumers (Streamlit dashboards reading parquet, LLM context injection) see wrong counts silently. Fix requires extending get_changed_files() to detect CSV-mtime > DuckDB-table-last-populated-time drift, OR adopting a persistent run log (#18's fix) that records what state each run propagated so subsequent runs can detect gaps. Design alongside #18 and #20 fixes — all three are symptoms of the same missing primitive (end_of_task state memory). Today's workaround: manual "dbt seed --full-refresh --select known_issues" to force propagation; bypasses the gate intentionally.
resolution: Fix landed prior to 2026-04-22 stabilization pass as scripts/end_of_task.py::get_stale_seeds() (attributed 'Fixes known_issue #21' in its docstring). get_changed_files() unions get_stale_seeds() output so post-commit CSV advance is detected via CSV-mtime > parquet-mtime comparison, independent of git-dirty state. Tracking row stayed open until 2026-04-22 audit. Verification (2026-04-22 commit 811cfd2): with known_decisions.csv mtime ~450s newer than its parquet, get_stale_seeds() flagged the file and get_changed_files() included it in its return — confirming the original incident scenario is covered.

title: scan_dbt_models outputs are non-deterministic across runs even without dbt model changes
description: Discovered 2026-04-19 during governance-gate fix step 4. scripts/scan_dbt_models.py produces non-deterministic output in two scanner seeds: (1) dbt_model_catalog.csv includes a last_updated timestamp column written at scan time, so every run rotates its sha256 even when no dbt source changed; (2) dbt_column_lineage.csv loses LLM-generated plain descriptions when scan runs in isolation, because those descriptions are refilled by sync_s2t_plain_from_dbt.py in a subsequent pipeline step (RULE 21 hash cache). Running the full end_of_task pipeline works correctly — the descriptions get restored — but content-hashing these seeds across runs is fundamentally unreliable. Impact: any content-hash based staleness system MUST treat dbt_column_lineage.csv and dbt_model_catalog.csv as derived/transient rather than as stable inputs. The governance-gate fix (step 4 today) resolved this for wiki sidecar by hashing the upstream dbt model SQL tree directly instead of the scanner output (Option 4 in the step 4 diagnosis). Two follow-up options for root-cause cleanup: (a) remove the timestamp column from dbt_model_catalog.csv — probably no downstream consumer needs it; requires grep audit first; (b) make scan_dbt_models.py preserve existing plain descriptions from the current seed when regenerating dbt_column_lineage.csv, so isolated scans don't strip them. Either would restore determinism to these seeds. Not urgent — the Option 4 workaround is stable — but worth cleanup when a future session has the scope.
resolution: Deferred — diff-noise only, no functional impact on data correctness. However: this non-determinism contributes to accumulated-uncommitted-file noise and was a contributor to the dcfc556 catch-all commit scenario (many uncommitted file diffs made narrow-scope commits impractical, forcing a 97-file full-accumulation commit). Future sessions should note: re-running scan_dbt_models mid-session can make an otherwise-clean working tree dirty without any semantic change. Fix requires stabilizing the timestamp + sort order in the scanner output; not in stabilization-pass scope.

title: source_column_roles RULE 37 floor needs bump after cold-start (10 -> ~280)
description: Constant lives at app/_csv_safeguard.py:53 inside the SAFEGUARDED_SEEDS['source_column_roles']['min_rows_absolute'] entry. Currently set to 10 as a bootstrap-safe floor so pre-cold-start states and partial runs (e.g. --table <small_table>) aren't blocked by the absolute-floor check in assert_csv_safe/assert_csv_safe_row_count. The classifier design specifies an intended steady-state floor of 495 (99% of an expected ~500-row seed). Trigger for the bump: the first successful cold-start run of scripts/classify_source_columns.py that completes stage 5 (atomic seed write), which will populate source_column_roles.csv with the actual observed row count. Once that row count is known, bump the floor to 99% of it (today's dry-run projects ~280-314 rows -> floor ~280). Also revisit the companion max_delete_per_op at _csv_safeguard.py:54 (currently 50); once stable, drop to something tighter like 10 to match the s2t_mapping precedent. Low priority: delta check already catches catastrophic truncation (existing N -> 0 raises when N > max_delete_per_op), so the loose floor is a defense-in-depth gap, not an active exposure.
resolution: cold-start completed 2026-04-19 (run 1: 50 LLM + 29 mechanical; run 2 --catchup: 235 LLM + 50 cache hits; total 314 cols / 41 tables, 0 fallbacks). Floor bumped to 310 at app/_csv_safeguard.py:53 (99% of observed 314). max_delete_per_op left at 50.

title: Hashdiff macro treats NULL and empty-string as identical, suppressing SCD2 expansion for ELIKZ-class nullable columns
description: Observed 2026-04-20 during the ELIKZ null-injection loop-closure validation (decision #71). Sequence: (a) Pre-injection raw_sap.ekpo had 2200 rows with ELIKZ uniformly empty string ''. (b) Injected 110 explicit NULLs into ELIKZ (5%) via deterministic selector. (c) main_staging.stg_sap__ekpo correctly reflected 110 NULL ELIKZ rows (staging is 1:1 passthrough). (d) Ran `dbt run --select sat_po_item` expecting SCD2 version expansion since ELIKZ is in the hashdiff input list (`hashdiff(['TXZ01', 'MENGE', 'MEINS', 'NETPR', 'NETWR', 'WERKS', 'LGORT', 'MATKL', 'PSTYP', 'ELIKZ'])` in stg_sap__ekpo.sql:28). (e) sat_po_item rowcount pre-run: 2200. Post-run: 2200. Zero delta. (f) Direct hashdiff comparison on a sample nulled row (EBELN=4500000020, EBELP=00010): staging.hashdiff_ekpo = 4ea68f016ef9499ec2a277ef04ab5faf (after NULL injection). sat_po_item.hashdiff for the same hk_po_material = 4ea68f016ef9499ec2a277ef04ab5faf (from original build). Identical. Hashdiff macro outputs the same hash for ELIKZ='' and ELIKZ=NULL. Root cause: the hash macro almost certainly applies COALESCE(x, '') or similar normalization before hashing, so null-vs-empty are indistinguishable at the hash layer. Downstream implication: any mart/OBT/knowledge metric that reads sat_po_item.delivery_completed_flag will not distinguish null-delivery from blank-delivery. A PO item that was freshly created (delivery not yet attempted → ELIKZ is really null) versus one with an explicit empty value (SAP default) collapses to the same vault row, losing a legitimate business signal. No fix proposed yet; logging for visibility. Options to consider later: (1) change hash macro to encode NULL as a distinct sentinel like '<NULL>' before concatenation; (2) add a separate is_null flag column to the satellite; (3) accept the semantics and document it in the business glossary.

title: raw_sap.* tables lack ingestion_date column - blocks age-based staleness UI (decision #72)
description: Decision #72 introduces age-based staleness using MAX(ingestion_date). Currently no raw_sap table has this column - data generator produces records without ingestion timestamps. Three paths forward: (a) modify seed generator to add ingestion_date to every raw_sap.* table; (b) add it opportunistically only to tables that accumulate historical data (e.g. mseg, vbap); (c) use a proxy (e.g. created_at if exists, or file mtime for seed-loaded data). Until resolved, DAR.last_source_ingestion_at will be NULL and UI will show 'age unknown' gracefully. Not blocking current analyzer work - is a blocker for the UI staleness feature.

title: Context assembler static layer loader silently returned 0 rows when seed schema mismatched query columns
description: Context-assembler verification discovery — scripts/_context_assembler.py:_load_static was issuing queries with incorrect column names against movement_type_mapping (used 'bwart'/'category' vs actual schema 'movement_type' without 'category'). The try/except swallowed DuckDB binder errors silently, producing {seed_name: 0} in static layer for every prior scope that queried it. Detected only because mseg was the first scope to read it. Same pattern is possible for other seeds if their schemas drift from the assembler's hardcoded SELECT. Proposed fix: schema introspection in _load_static — SELECT * and handle output columns dynamically; OR: pin each seed's expected schema in a module-level dict with dbt test that fails if actual seed schema drifts from declared expectation. Not blocking current analyzer work. Status note: the movement_type_mapping-specific symptom was resolved ad hoc during that verification by updating the SELECT to the correct column names. The broader pattern (silent try/except hiding binder errors) remains — same issue can recur for any other seed if its schema evolves.
resolution: Resolved in a maintenance pass: _load_static now narrows swallowed exceptions via _loud_fail_or_swallow helper that detects BinderException / CatalogException / ParserException signatures and prints WARN to stderr. Env var PIECE8_STATIC_LOADER_DIAGNOSTIC=1 forces raise for debug/reproduction. 7 try/except blocks in _load_static updated; behavior for IO/missing-seed unchanged.

title: Code Tables auto-picker heuristic — 'lowest cardinality wins' without lower bound picks trivial single-value columns
description: The first verification run auto-picked MEINS (1 distinct value) over BWART because _auto_pick_code_column used 'lowest cardinality wins' with no minimum. A 1-distinct column is trivially lowest but useless for Code Tables — no meaningful mapping possible. Ad-hoc fix applied: cardinality window [2, 50] + explicit BWART preference. Principled fix would make auto-picker read source_column_roles + check description-source coverage in bundle before picking, instead of relying on cardinality heuristic alone. DAR-00008 kept as forensic evidence of the pre-fix run. Not blocking; decision #72 staleness is informational so DAR-00008 stays as valid audit record.
resolution: Resolved: _auto_pick_code_column now preserves cardinality window [2, 50] AND prefers columns whose name matches a convention-discovered decoder code column or ends with _CODE / _STATUS. Backward-compat BWART preference retained. Combined with the decoder-discovery fix, the auto-picker now correctly biases toward real code columns (APPR_STATUS, REASON_CODE) over other dimensions (GJAHR).

title: Per-term regression harness leaves compile artifacts in shared dbt/models/ tree; cross-term contamination produces false compile failures
description: The Create S2T A/B test exposed a latent bug present in the earlier migration regression harness too: the scratch folder lives under dbt/models/, so compile artifacts from a failed/completed term persist across the next term compile step. BG001 NEW fact_purchase_orders.sql leftover produced identical duplicate-model-name compile errors for BG027 OLD, BG027 NEW, BG007 OLD, BG007 NEW — none of which actually compiled their own generated SQL. The earlier regression did not hit this because none of its terms chose a production-model name. Fix: clear _scratch/ entirely at start of every term (not just between paths of same term), OR run compile with --target=temp_scratch and isolate via dbt profile. Not blocking migration closure (token efficiency evidence unaffected).
resolution: Legacy migration regression harnesses are superseded by scripts/regression_piece8.py which uses the scratch-folder wipe pattern described in the issue's resolution sketch. No further action needed; the cross-term contamination class no longer exists in the active harness.

title: Create S2T directives cover 4 dynamic sources but not ontology layer — LLM can pick production-model names and create duplicates
description: The Create S2T A/B test showed the new path on BG001 generated a model named fact_purchase_orders, which already exists in dbt/models/marts/fact_purchase_orders.sql. The bundle ontology layer contained the existing_models list — LLM had access to it — but picked a colliding name. Current prompt directives cover domain_facts, analysis_findings, DAR rows, BAR rows (4 dynamic sources). Missing: explicit ontology consumption directive. Need addition: If the bundle ontology layer contains an existing model with a name matching your proposed dbt_models[i].name, you MUST use ref(existing_model_name) instead of creating a duplicate. Naming collision is a failure. Add to the term-analysis runner's context integrity safeguards — ontology_consumed attestation field.

title: Term-analysis reasoning layer blocked on approved design doc
description: The context-assembly migration closed (decision #76 + #77). The pre-S2T reasoning layer (the term-analysis runner) is the next implementation work. Do NOT begin coding until user provides the approved design doc. Timing-sensitive companion: delete OLD Create S2T fallback _create_s2t_with_implementation_OLD_PRE_P72 from app/claude_api.py after 2026-04-26 (decision #76 one-week green prod window). Known issues blocking or shaping the reasoning layer: #25, #26, #27, #28 (MUST fix harness before its regression suite), #29 (ontology directive MUST be in its safeguards).
resolution: Resolved — the term-analysis reasoning layer is fully implemented. This known_issue was a historical gating placeholder for work that has since been completed; no longer relevant. Closed in a maintenance pass.

title: DuckDB connection config mismatch across callers in same process — latent in multiple other spots
description: Multiple duckdb.connect() calls with different config (read_only=True vs read-write, or different memory_limit) in the same Python process against the same .duckdb file fail with 'Can't open a connection to same database file with a different configuration than existing connections'. Surfaced 2026-04-20 during term-analysis runner live verification — runner opened read-write conn at scripts/run_term_injection.py:738 and _context_assembler.py:914 internally opened read_only=True, causing the live run to crash between step 1 and step 2. Fix pattern (applied to the runner): pass conn as parameter rather than opening new connections inside called functions. Latent in at least these other spots that future integration work may trip on: app/db.py:335 (read_only=False context), app/claude_api.py:577 (read_only=True context — _resolve_term_scope), scripts/_sidecar.py:264 + :317 (both read_only=True), scripts/run_magnitude_analysis.py:170 (read-write). Not blocking the term-analysis runner (already fixed there); flag for staged-pipeline integration work when UI-triggered runs may chain these modules in one process.

title: Citation audit threshold needs calibration — temporary allowance of 20 unknowns, design target 0
description: scripts/run_term_injection.py:_grep_audit is the term-analysis runner's structured citation audit safeguard. Design target: zero unknowns trigger hard_stop_citation_audit_failure. Live verification discovered that real LLM SQL generates 2-10 'unknown' tokens per iteration from legitimate SAP idiom (mixed-case column names like BEWTP or JULIANDAY that aren't in the SQL-keyword allowlist but ARE valid). Raised threshold to env var PIECE8_CITATION_AUDIT_THRESHOLD (default 20) as a pragmatic workaround for verification. Proper fix (deferred): AST-based SQL parser that distinguishes table refs / column refs / function calls / literals; threshold can then legitimately be 0 for table+column classes while allowing DuckDB builtin functions and SAP field conventions through. Alternative: expand the _SQL_KEYWORDS allowlist with common SAP field names and DuckDB function names.
resolution: Resolved: _grep_audit replaced with _ast_audit — sqlparse-based tokenizer + DuckDB information_schema.columns validation + sap_data_dictionary fallback + FN_ALLOWLIST for DuckDB builtins + citation-ID span detection + string-literal stripping (position-preserving). Threshold=0 design target achieved. PIECE8_CITATION_AUDIT_THRESHOLD env var removed. 8/8 AST-audit unit tests pass.

title: Term-analysis runner — AST audit SELECT-alias false positive + trace not persisted on hard-stop paths
description: Two bugs discovered during live BG001 verification 2026-04-20, both since fixed: (1) _ast_audit flagged SELECT-list output aliases as unknown columns — e.g. `SELECT AVG(x) AS avg_vendor_lead_time FROM ...` had `avg_vendor_lead_time` flagged because it wasn't in sap_data_dictionary or information_schema.columns. Fixed via _OUTPUT_ALIAS_PATTERN regex + skip in bare-identifier validation pass. Also hardened CTE detection: old _CTE_PATTERN non-greedy match stopped at first SELECT inside first CTE, missing subsequent CTEs in multi-CTE queries; replaced with broad `<name> AS (` scan across entire SQL. CTE-qualified refs (e.g. `g.gr_date` where `g` resolves to a CTE) now skip column validation since CTE output columns can't be checked against information_schema. (2) Hard-stop exit paths (citation_audit_failure, ontology_collision, attestation_failure, oscillation, reflection LLM errors) did `break` out of iteration loop without appending to iteration_trace, producing iterations_count=0 and iteration_trace=[] on failed runs even though iteration 1 had actually run. Also scope_tables wasn't persisted in step-14 UPDATE payload. Fixed by appending partial trace entry at every hard-stop path with gates_result indicating specific failure (e.g. {'citation_audit': 'fail', 'unknown_refs': [...]}) and including scope_tables in updates dict. Verification: live BG001 run produced iter_count=1, scope_tables=['ekbe','ekko','mseg'], trace_len=1.
resolution: Fixed via ~90 LOC changes in scripts/run_term_injection.py: _OUTPUT_ALIAS_PATTERN, rewritten _extract_cte_names, prefix-CTE skip in qualified-col validation, trace-append at 5 hard-stop exit paths, scope_tables in step-14 updates.

title: Layer A semantic model — EDA-compiled conventions for raw tables without dbt ontology coverage
description: The design introduces Layer A: a new dbt/seeds/semantic_model.csv compiled from the EDA framework (DAR reservoir) that teaches the LLM canonical per-table SQL-writing conventions (canonical_alias, primary_key_cols, typical_join_keys_json, code_column_refs_json, typical_filters, common_traps, reference_sql). Motivation: two live BG001 runs on 2026-04-21 produced divergent outcomes — BAR-00001 converged at \$0.203 when the LLM sampled columns in the project sample data (VGABE, BWART), BAR-00002 hard_stopped on hard_stop_citation_audit_failure at \$0.131 when the LLM picked BEWTP (valid SAP column, absent from project sample data). Layer A addresses the root by grounding per-table conventions in observed EDA data rather than LLM SAP priors. Universal mechanism: compiled from the four EDA analyses (Completeness, Dimensions, Magnitude, Code Tables), not hand-authored; generalizes to any source. Consumer priority: ontology first (dbt_column_lineage.origin_table) then Layer A. Landed artifacts: dbt/seeds/semantic_model.csv (17 cols) + schema.yml column_types block + _csv_safeguard entry; scripts/compile_semantic_model.py (~350 LOC) + scripts/prompts/semantic_model_compilation_prompt.md; scripts/trigger_layer_a_compile.py (Case B manual CLI with DAR-completeness self-diagnostic — no pipeline driver currently chains the four EDA analyzers); _context_assembler.py amendment (LAYER_SOURCE_CSVS + EMPTY_SOURCES + _load_static Layer A block + conditional semantic_model_coverage_gap signal); term_injection_iteration/reflection/finalization prompt directives + ATTESTATION_FIELDS += semantic_model_consumed; app/claude_api.py Create S2T directive 3e; regression_piece8.py scenario 15 (R, LLM-dependent).
resolution: Implemented per the design. Human-override rows (populated_by=human_override OR review_state=human_reviewed) preserved across recompile. Ontology-first-Layer-A-second consumer priority enforced. Pipeline-driver migration path documented in trigger_layer_a_compile.py docstring (dbt post-hook automation deferred as post-launch hardening).

title: Layer B dbt semantic model — deterministically compiled from manifest.json for dbt-covered scopes
description: The design introduces Layer B: dbt/seeds/dbt_semantic_model.csv compiled deterministically from dbt/target/manifest.json via scripts/compile_dbt_semantic_model.py — no LLM synthesis (manifest is structured ground truth). 14 columns per model: model_name (PK), dbt_layer (classified by name prefix), materialized, upstream_models, downstream_models (reverse-lookup), exposed_columns_json (from node.columns + test cross-reference), primary_key_cols, canonical_alias, typical_join_keys_json, reference_sql (stored in dbt canonical Jinja form), model_description, populated_at_utc, populated_by, source_manifest_hash (staleness detection). Motivation: Layer A covers raw tables without ontology; Layer B covers dbt-modeled tables. Together they form consumer priority: Layer B first (dbt-covered) → Layer A second (raw-only) → base LLM third. Dual rendering: assembler rewrites {{ ref() }} → main_<schema>.<model> literal form for iteration consumers (purpose=pre_s2t_reasoning — mechanical gate runs raw DuckDB, no Jinja); preserves Jinja for Create S2T consumers (purpose=create_s2t — dbt renders at compile time). Schema mapping parsed from dbt_project.yml models section with main_<layer> fallback per RULE 11. Landed artifacts: dbt/seeds/dbt_semantic_model.csv + schema.yml + SAFEGUARDED_SEEDS; scripts/compile_dbt_semantic_model.py (~340 LOC, deterministic); scripts/_context_assembler.py (LAYER_SOURCE_CSVS/EMPTY_SOURCES additions + dbt_layer→schema parser + _load_static Layer B block with dual-rendering + dbt_semantic_model_coverage_gap conditional + manifest.json hash in fingerprint); term_injection_iteration/reflection/finalization prompts + ATTESTATION_FIELDS += dbt_semantic_model_consumed (7 total); app/claude_api.py DIRECTIVE 3f (Layer B — Jinja-preserving for Create S2T); regression_piece8.py scenario 16 (R, LLM-dependent); _make_attestation + scenario 13 fixture updated for 7-field contract.
resolution: Implemented per the design. Dual rendering preserves architectural integrity (literal refs for iteration) while enabling dbt-native output for Create S2T. Human-override rows preserved across recompile. No auto-trigger — manual CLI only (parallel to Layer A); post-hook automation deferred as post-launch hardening.

title: BAR schema missed semantic_model_consumed + dbt_semantic_model_consumed columns when Layer A/B landed — silent attestation data loss
description: The Layer A work extended ATTESTATION_FIELDS in scripts/run_term_injection.py with semantic_model_consumed; the Layer B work extended it again with dbt_semantic_model_consumed. Both extensions shipped prompt directives + attestation_complete validation + assembler source rendering BUT neither added the matching persistence columns to the BAR schema (business_term_analysis_results). Live BG001 diagnostic afterwards showed runs converged correctly but querying BAR row for Layer A/B attestation returned nothing — columns did not exist. Same class of silent data-loss as the earlier iteration_trace/iterations_count hard-stop bug (different trigger, same root: partially-extended write path). The design doc now codifies a 7-step checklist for future attestation extensions so the full migration lands atomically.
resolution: Fixed: (a) dbt/seeds/business_term_analysis_results.csv header +2 cols; 1 existing data row (BAR-00001) backfilled with ,[],[] appended; (b) dbt/seeds/schema.yml BAR column_types block +2 varchar entries; (c) scripts/_bar_writer.py BAR_COLUMNS list +2 entries at end (migration-safe append); (d) scripts/run_term_injection.py step-14 update payload +2 json.dumps(finalize.get(...)) lines; (e) _synthesize_finalization_from_trace budget-exhaustion fallback path extended with 2 union accumulators so both paths produce the 7-field shape; (f) regression scenarios 15 and 16 extended with BAR-column assertions alongside existing trace-level checks; (g) known_issues #36 entry; (h) design-doc checklist amendment. BAR schema count 37 → 39.

title: Second-wave EDA enrichment — 4 new DAR types + Layer A schema extension
description: The design introduces a second wave of EDA enrichment: 4 new analysis_type values on main_seeds.domain_analysis_results (temporal_coverage, performance_baseline, grain_relationship, segmentation_threshold) + 4 new VARCHAR JSON columns on semantic_model (Layer A): temporal_coverage_json, typical_values_range_json, grain_relationships_json, natural_thresholds_json. Motivation: original 4 analyzers (Completeness/Dimensions/Magnitude/Code Tables) cover population/cardinality/volume/decoding dimensions but miss temporal coverage, measure baselines, grain relationships, and natural thresholds — all useful for Layer A convention-grounding of LLM SQL generation for future raw-only scopes (new sources without dbt ontology). Deterministic analyzers (no LLM calls) emit DARs via pure SQL aggregation. grain_relationships_json shape locked: self-contained per-row list with other_table+role+detail_col+header_col+sum_match_pct+confidence, symmetric entries on co-compile. Segmentation MVP uses quartile thresholds; KDE deferred. Compilation via existing trigger_layer_a_compile.py + compile_semantic_model.py (now queries all 8 DAR types). Artifacts: scripts/run_date_analysis.py (NEW ~230 LOC); scripts/run_magnitude_analysis.py extension (+120 LOC performance_baseline co-emission); scripts/run_grain_relationship_analysis.py (NEW ~340 LOC); scripts/run_segmentation_analysis.py (NEW ~230 LOC); compile_semantic_model.py (+70 LOC new-DAR gather + mock support); prompts/semantic_model_compilation_prompt.md (18 emitted keys, enrichment synthesis guidance); Layer A schema.yml + CSV +4 columns; DAR accepted_values extended; regression scenarios 17-20.
resolution: Implemented per the design. 4 analyzers emit DARs into main_seeds.domain_analysis_results; compile_semantic_model.py co-compiles into Layer A rows via 4 new JSON fields. PIECE8_COMPILE_MOCK_RESPONSE env var enables non-LLM regression (scenarios 17-20 mock-backed). The 7-step attestation checklist followed per new field. Out of scope: Change-Over-Time, Cumulative Analysis, Ranking, hand-curated SQL idiom library. Pipeline-driver hardening deferred. Layer B unchanged (dbt metadata already structured).

title: Demo model expanded with ZMM_APPROVAL_LOG Z-table + BG028 three-way matching term
description: Landed raw_sap.zmm_approval_log (500 rows, 12 cols SGTXT-not-COMMENT per SQL keyword avoidance) — first raw-only table in the project with zero dbt coverage. Created 2 decoder seeds (zmm_approval_status, zmm_reason_codes) + 12 sap_data_dictionary rows + 12 source_column_roles rows as deterministic gap-fills so EDA analyzers have column semantics without LLM classification. Ran full 8-analyzer EDA pipeline (3 deterministic enrichment analyzers first then 4 LLM analyzers + 2 explicit Code Tables runs for APPR_STATUS/REASON_CODE) producing 9 DARs for zmm_approval_log. Compiled Layer A — first real semantic_model row in project history with all 21 cols populated (canonical_alias=zal, entity_class=fact, enrichment fields carrying TOL_AMT stats + APPR_DATE span). Created BG028 po_invoice_three_way_match_variance glossary term (7-table scope: ekko/ekpo/ekbe/rbkp/rseg/lfa1/zmm_approval_log — exercises Layer A + Layer B + complex aggregation). Live term-analysis run (max-iters 8, budget-cap 2.00) hard_stopped on iter 1 with convergence_reason=hard_stop_citation_audit_failure: AST audit flagged alias:0 + alias:100 (regex false-positive from numeric literals / CAST type args in the LLM-proposed SQL). Cost 0.29/2.00. Layer A attestation correctly populated (semantic_model_consumed=[zmm_approval_log]); Layer B attestation correctly populated (dbt_semantic_model_consumed=[stg_sap__ekko,stg_sap__ekpo,stg_sap__ekbe,stg_sap__rseg]). Architecture failed-safe as designed; trace persisted. The demo script captured the full flow.
resolution: Landed in the demo-model expansion that added BG028 (see commit dcfc556). raw_sap.zmm_approval_log + 2 decoder seeds + BG028 three-way matching term + fact_po_invoice_match_variance mart all shipped. This tracking row simply never got flipped to resolved; doing so now.

title: AST _OUTPUT_ALIAS_PATTERN false-positive on numeric tokens from CAST type args
description: Discovered during live BG028 verification: AST audit hard-stopped on iter 1 with unknowns ['alias:0', 'alias:100']. Root cause: _OUTPUT_ALIAS_PATTERN = re.compile(r'\bAS\s+(\w+)') captured numeric tokens from CAST type arguments like CAST(x AS DECIMAL(13,2)) where the digits 0 and 2 got matched as 'alias names'. Real SQL output aliases never begin with a digit.
resolution: Tightened regex to r'\bAS\s+([a-zA-Z_]\w*)'. Added tests/test_ast_alias_pattern.py with 8 unit-test cases including CAST(x AS DECIMAL(13,2)) AS metric, CAST(y AS INTEGER) AS counter, and regression cases for avg_x / vendor_id / month. 8/8 pass. BG028 no longer hard-stops on this class of false-positive.

title: Code Tables analyzer decoder-source allowlist hardcoded — now convention-discovered
description: Discovered during the demo-model expansion: Code Tables analyzer returned description_source='none' for zmm_approval_log's APPR_STATUS + REASON_CODE code columns even though decoder seeds zmm_approval_status / zmm_reason_codes existed. Root cause: analyzer's hardcoded allowlist only knew about movement_type_mapping / t156 / sap_data_dictionary. New decoder seeds required manual allowlist extension per seed.
resolution: Added _discover_decoder_candidates(conn) that queries main_seeds at runtime for decoder-shaped seeds (<=500 rows, 2-4 cols, has code-convention column + description-convention column). Injected into code_tables_analysis_prompt.md via new {decoder_candidates} placeholder. Re-run on zmm_approval_log: APPR_STATUS now maps to zmm_approval_status; REASON_CODE maps to zmm_reason_codes. Backward-compat list retained for SAP-standard decoders.

title: Layer A compile prompt citation discipline insufficient — strengthened
description: Discovered during the Layer A compile for zmm_approval_log: source_dar_ids cited only 3 of 7+ DARs the LLM actually read. Dual root cause: (a) compile prompt did not mandate citing every DAR read; (b) legacy DAR types (completeness/dimensions/magnitude/code_tables) were injected as bare result_json without their DAR IDs, so the LLM literally couldn't see the IDs to cite them.
resolution: Added explicit CITATION DISCIPLINE section to semantic_model_compilation_prompt.md with worked example. Complementary fix in compile_semantic_model.py: legacy DAR injection now wraps each finding with its DAR id (_legacy_bundle helper returning {dar_id, finding}) so the LLM sees the ID it needs to cite. Recompile for zmm_approval_log now cites all 7 DARs consumed.

title: Trace gates_result missing attestation fields — companion to the BAR schema fix
description: Discovered during live BG028 diagnostic: BAR-level semantic_model_consumed / dbt_semantic_model_consumed columns populate correctly (per the earlier BAR schema fix), but per-iteration iteration_trace[i].gates_result did NOT carry these fields. Post-hoc analysis of which Layer A / Layer B rows were cited in iteration N specifically was impossible — only the session-level union was available.
resolution: Fixed: _trace_entry extended with semantic_model_consumed / dbt_semantic_model_consumed kwargs; gates_result dict now includes both. Iteration loop extracts from propose response once per iteration (iter_sm_consumed / iter_dsm_consumed) and threads through every _trace_entry call (happy path + all 7 hard-stop paths). Scenarios 15/16 extended to assert presence. Companion to the BAR schema extension.

title: Create S2T consumer for promoted BARs landed — pipeline closure term -> BAR -> dbt model
description: This work closes the end-to-end pipeline: when a BAR is promoted (status='promoted', human-gated per anti-pattern #18), create_s2t_with_implementation in app/claude_api.py now dispatches through a new BAR-consumer path (create_s2t_from_promoted_bar) instead of regenerating SQL from scratch. The promoted BAR's final_query_sql is authoritative; the translation task only rewrites literal `main_<schema>.<model>` references to Jinja {{ ref() }} form, adds materialization config, and derives dbt tests + schema.yml description from term_conditions_covered + final_metric_interpretation. Post-translation audit enforces every emitted ref() is in BAR's dbt_semantic_model_consumed or Layer B. Fallback path (generator) preserved unchanged for terms without promoted BARs. Live demo verified end-to-end: BG028 promoted BAR -> generated fact_po_invoice_match_variance dbt model -> dbt run PASS -> dbt test PASS -> 184 materialized rows (90.48% avg three-way match rate, 105/135 approved exceptions) delivering plausible business numbers from a full 7-table scope including the Layer A-covered zmm_approval_log Z-table.
resolution: Implemented per the design. Landed artifacts: scripts/_bar_consumer.py (PromotedBarInput + resolve_promoted_bar, ~200 LOC); scripts/prompts/create_s2t_from_bar_prompt.md (translation prompt, ~100 LOC); app/claude_api.py dispatcher + create_s2t_from_promoted_bar + regex ref-audit (~200 LOC); scripts/verify_bar_promotion.py diagnostic (~80 LOC); scripts/run_create_s2t.py CLI wrapper (~110 LOC); scripts/regression_piece8.py scenario 21 (LLM-dep, ~100 LOC); dbt/models/marts/fact_po_invoice_match_variance.sql + schema.yml entry (live-demo materialization). Streamlit UI contract preserved (new `source` + `bar_id` top-level keys are additive). Total LOC: ~800 under the 400-600 soft estimate (materialization artifact adds to the spread). Cost: $0.455 term-analysis re-run + $0.14 Create S2T translation + $0.14 scenario 21 = ~$0.74 total.

title: SAP date-name heuristic missed *DT suffix (eket.EINDT uncovered by run_date_analysis)
description: Discovered during the BG002 EDA bootstrap: run_date_analysis.py skipped eket entirely because its date column EINDT ends in DT (not DAT/DATE/DATUM). Other SAP tables with *DT date columns (eban.FRGDT, eqbs.BEGDT, equi.INBDT) were also silently missed. MANDT (client code) matches the same suffix pattern but is NOT a date, so a naive relaxation would introduce false-positives.
resolution: Added _SAP_DT_SUFFIX_PATTERN (r'^[A-Z]{2,4}DT$') with _SAP_DT_BLOCKLIST={'MANDT'} to _is_sap_date_column_name in scripts/run_date_analysis.py. Verified on eket: EINDT now produces DAR-00057 (temporal_coverage: 2024-01-28 to 2026-03-25, 787d span, 0 gaps, 0% null). MANDT stays blocked. EDIT/DEBIT/CREDIT not matched (prefix length > 4 or doesn't end in DT). ~10 LOC.

title: run_code_tables_analysis hard-errored on tables with no source_column_roles entries
description: Discovered during the BG002 EDA bootstrap: run_code_tables_analysis.py errored with '[error] no dimension-role columns on raw_sap.eket' when source_column_roles had no entries for eket. Any new scope table added via Stage A would trigger this hard-error, blocking analyzer invocation before source_column_roles population completes.
resolution: Added _discover_dimension_candidates_heuristic fallback that discovers candidate columns from information_schema when source_column_roles is empty for a table. Match criteria: VARCHAR with character_maximum_length <= 10, OR name ends with _STATUS/_TYPE/_CODE/_KEY/_IND/_FLAG/_CATEGORY, OR name is in _CODE_COL_SAP_NAMES (BWART, AUART, etc.). Cardinality window [2,50] filter applied same as roles-based path. Analyzer now exits cleanly with 'no dimension candidates found' info message when neither path yields candidates. Verified on eket: clean exit (eket has 3 keys + 1 date + 2 quantities, genuinely no code columns). ~60 LOC.

title: AST _FROM_JOIN_PATTERN over-matched EXTRACT/TRIM FROM col as table references
description: Discovered during BG002 term-analysis iteration 1 verification: citation_audit_failure hard-stop surfaced unknown_refs: ['table:goods_receipt_date']. Root cause: _FROM_JOIN_PATTERN regex matched 'FROM' inside EXTRACT(QUARTER FROM m.goods_receipt_date), classifying the column as a table reference. Third AST regex false-positive class after the earlier _OUTPUT_ALIAS_PATTERN and _QUALIFIED_COL_PATTERN fixes.
resolution: Added _FUNCTION_FROM_PATTERN (matches EXTRACT/TRIM/SUBSTRING arg spans with inner FROM) + _strip_function_from_clauses helper that blanks those spans (length-preserving) before _FROM_JOIN_PATTERN runs. Added tests/test_ast_from_join_pattern.py with 8 cases covering EXTRACT(YEAR FROM date_col), EXTRACT(QUARTER FROM m.goods_receipt_date), TRIM(LEADING ' ' FROM col), SUBSTRING(name FROM 1 FOR 3), and genuine FROM/JOIN refs. 8/8 pass. Existing test_ast_alias_pattern.py still 8/8 pass (no regression). Known limitation: one level of parenthesis nesting; deeper nesting falls through to status-quo match. ~30 LOC + ~90 LOC tests.

title: z_tables_catalog static-layer loader queried non-existent business_purpose column
description: Flagged by the loud-fail helper on every Create S2T invocation: static-layer loader in scripts/_context_assembler.py queried 'SELECT table_name, description, business_purpose FROM main_seeds.z_tables_catalog' but the seed's actual columns are table_name, description, important_fields, maintenance_transaction, rows_estimate. Binder error returned 0 rows on every scope, silently losing Z-table context from the bundle. Logged as an observation during the maintenance pass; fixed shortly after.
resolution: Dropped 'business_purpose' from the SELECT and from the _csv_fmt rendered header. New query is 'SELECT table_name, description FROM main_seeds.z_tables_catalog' - description already captures purpose for Create S2T context. Verified: Code Tables run on eket no longer emits the loud-fail warning. No direct substitute chosen since the other available columns (important_fields, rows_estimate) are semantically different from business_purpose. ~4 LOC.

title: Trace gates_result attestation could silently drop at BAR write
description: Discovered during completion review of the trace-attestation fix: BAR-level semantic_model_consumed / dbt_semantic_model_consumed columns read from finalize.get(...) directly. If the LLM's finalization response omitted citations present in trace iterations (common when finalization budget is tight), BAR silently lost those citations even though trace[N].gates_result preserved them. Also: _union_field in _synthesize_finalization_from_trace read from it.get('response') which is never populated on trace entries (no-op union). Scenarios 15/16 'response'-key reads were broken for the same reason.
resolution: Added _union_attestation_from_trace_and_finalize(iteration_trace, finalize, field) helper that unions finalize.<field> + every trace[N].gates_result.<field>. BAR-level writes for semantic_model_consumed and dbt_semantic_model_consumed now route through this helper, guaranteeing BAR.<field> is a superset of every trace[N].gates_result.<field>. _union_field in _synthesize_finalization_from_trace also extended to read gates_result for Layer A/B fields. Scenarios 15/16 updated: read from gates_result instead of non-existent 'response' key, assert BAR-is-superset-of-trace[0] invariant. Added tests/test_bar_attestation_trace_union.py with 7 unit tests covering single-iter / multi-iter / finalize-drops / finalize-only / empty / layer B / order-preserving dedup. 7/7 pass. ~40 LOC + ~90 LOC tests.

title: sap_data_dictionary backfilled to full 326-row coverage (42 raw_sap tables)
description: Prior state: 57 hand-authored rows covering 9 tables (33 raw_sap tables had zero coverage, 8 partial, 1 full). Stage A depends on column-level business semantics; pre-backfill gap would have forced Stage A to reason from column names alone. Additionally: sap_data_dictionary was not in SAFEGUARDED_SEEDS, leaving it vulnerable to catastrophic truncation (parallel to the 2026-04 s2t_mapping incident that motivated the safeguard mechanism).
resolution: Extended schema with 2 new columns (description_source, needs_review) via dbt/seeds/schema.yml + CSV header rewrite. Implemented scripts/run_catalog_backfill.py (~330 LOC) — per-table LLM batching via claude-sonnet-4 with 5m prompt caching on the system prompt + directives block (cache_read 63200 tokens across 40 calls). 5-value description_source enum: sap_standard (253) / column_name_convention (4) / source_column_roles (12) / inferred (0) / existing (57). Zero 'inferred' rows is notable — LLM claimed full confidence across all 269 generated cells; Stage A consumers should apply their own confidence signals rather than relying on needs_review=1 as a coverage indicator. Validation: 0/269 rejections; 326/326 total rows; all 42 tables match information_schema column counts exactly; ground truth (MARD existing cells, ZMM_APPROVAL_LOG full) preserved with description_source='existing'. Cost: $0.7296 (wall 12.9 min, 40 API calls + 1 cache hit). SAFEGUARDED_SEEDS extended to include sap_data_dictionary (min_rows=300, max_delete=10). Per-table response cache at dbt/seeds/.catalog_backfill_cache/ (gitignored) enables resumable re-runs. One known edge: SERI.ANESSION_DATE got source=column_name_convention but blank data_type (validator only enforced type-required for sap_standard, not convention) - policy gap, low risk, flagged for future hardening.

title: sap_data_dictionary backfill produced zero inferred rows - LLM self-confidence uncalibrated
description: The catalog backfill produced 269 generated rows, all claimed description_source in {sap_standard, column_name_convention, source_column_roles} with needs_review=0. Zero rows flagged as 'inferred'. LLM self-confidence is uncalibrated - not a calibrated coverage-gap signal. Stage A and downstream consumers should NOT rely on needs_review=1 to identify low-confidence catalog cells. Potential future work: periodically sample the catalog for analyst spot-check, or rerun with stricter prompt directives that require 'inferred' source for Z-prefix columns or columns with no role-tag signal.
resolution: Deferred — no systematic fix available. LLM cannot be forced to admit uncertainty on command; the prompt directive requesting 'inferred' for low-confidence rows was not honored on any of 269 generated rows. Risk: confidently-wrong descriptions for non-standard fields. SERI.ANESSION_DATE was an example we caught by luck (typo in generator propagated to catalog via column_name_convention path, and analyst spotted it in an unrelated diagnostic). Mitigation: spot-check suspicious rows during review — especially Z-prefix (HT-custom) columns and any field with unusual naming. Accepted limitation, not a silent bug (the inaccuracy is observable when used); tracked here so future sap_data_dictionary revisits can schedule review passes.

title: raw_sap.seri.ANESSION_DATE renamed to ACCESSION_DATE - sample data typo fixed
description: The sample data generator (scripts/generate_sap_sample_data.py L802) created a SERI table with column name ANESSION_DATE - a typo for ACCESSION_DATE. SERI is an invented sample table (not standard SAP we inherited); the typo propagated through raw_sap.seri (8777 rows), stg_sap__seri staging model, sap_data_dictionary catalog, source_column_roles, and dbt_column_lineage. Surfaced during catalog backfill verification (column fell into column_name_convention source without a data_type because LLM was appropriately conservative about the unusual field name).
resolution: Pre-Stage-A cleanup pass: renamed throughout the stack. Generator L802 typo fix -> ALTER TABLE raw_sap.seri RENAME COLUMN (atomic, 8777 rows preserved) -> stg_sap__seri.sql updated -> sap_data_dictionary CSV row renamed (also populated data_type=DATE length=8 since rename promotes the row from 'LLM conservative' to 'clear convention') -> source_column_roles CSV row renamed -> dbt_column_lineage CSV row renamed -> reseeded all 3 affected seeds via --full-refresh -> rebuilt stg_sap__seri view -> grep-verified zero ANESSION hits in live code (only remaining reference is in known_issue #49 resolution text as historical record, intentionally preserved).

title: Stage A (LLM-driven scope derivation) landed - Data Analysis Term Scope tab
description: BG002 verification (pre-commit dcfc556) exposed an architectural gap: pre-authored s2t_mapping scopes were incomplete (BG002 had only eket+mkpf, lacking the ekko/ekpo/ekbe/mseg bridge chain), causing term-analysis hard-stops on citation audit. The term-analysis runner consumes scope; does not derive it. The gap was in the upstream assumption that analysts authored scope correctly at term-creation time. Stage A closes the gap with an LLM-driven derivation step between draft and approved.
resolution: Stage A: new Data Analysis tab '🧭 Term Scope' (FIRST tab; default landing changed). Workflow: draft term -> Propose Scope button -> LLM proposes tables + rationale + blockers + attestation -> analyst revises via composed re-prompt (add/remove/explain/free-text) -> Confirm writes s2t_mapping rows + sets status=scope_confirmed + stamps scope_derivation_history_json. Schema extended: business_glossary +1 column (scope_derivation_history_json) + accepted_values enum +5 values (scope_confirmed, domain_eda_pending, term_eda_pending, ready_for_s2t kept as forward-compat; only scope_confirmed wired by Stage A). Prerequisites readout shows per-table domain EDA status + deep-link-style instructions to Guided Analysis - Domain tab (Stage B/C deferred; term_eda status='not_applicable_yet' for now). Proof via regression scenario 22 (LLM-dep, $0.06): BG002 reset to draft, Stage A proposed {ekko, ekpo, eket, ekbe, mkpf, mseg} with confidence=high and zero blockers, confirmed cleanly, prerequisites readout worked, BG002 restored to approved state post-test. UI observable change: default Data Analysis landing is now Term Scope not Guided Analysis - Business Term; users navigating by habit will see the new tab first. Known limitations: approved-status terms cannot be re-derived via Stage A (must reset to draft first - intentional guard against clobbering curated scopes); Streamlit tab deep-linking not supported (next_steps hints are instructional text, not clickable). Stages B (domain EDA refactor) and C (term EDA) deferred to future sessions.

title: Parquet-backed Streamlit views diverge from DuckDB after seed-writers - Stage A confirm_scope fixed, other writers deferred
description: Surfaced during Stage A live-dashboard verification. Architecture: app/db.py opens an in-memory DuckDB and registers every data/parquet/<schema>/<table>.parquet as a view (deliberately avoids opening cpe_analytics.duckdb to prevent Windows file-lock contention with dbt). Any seed writer that updates cpe_analytics.duckdb via dbt seed but skips the parquet export step leaves the Streamlit view catalog stale. Stage A confirm_scope initially did only dbt seed -> DuckDB had scope_derivation_history_json column, parquet did not -> Streamlit views failed with BinderError on scope_derivation_history_json references. Root cause is architectural divergence, not a one-off bug: any write path that doesn't call both {dbt seed OR direct DB write} AND export_parquet.py AND invalidate the view catalog will exhibit the same symptom.
resolution: Partial fix: scripts/_scope_derivation.py::confirm_scope now runs scripts/export_parquet.py as subprocess after dbt seed succeeds, then calls app.db.close_connection() to invalidate the Streamlit view catalog. Parquet export failure logs a warning but does not roll back (DuckDB + CSV are source of truth; parquet is derived). Scenario 22 regression passes export_parquet=False because its restore path would re-export wastefully. Follow-up needed: audit all other seed-writing paths for the same class of divergence. Candidates: BAR promotion (app/claude_api.py + app/pages/Business_Glossary.py), Create S2T consumer (scripts/_bar_consumer.py), Layer A/B compile (scripts/compile_semantic_model.py, scripts/compile_dbt_semantic_model.py), term-analysis BAR writes (scripts/_bar_writer.py), EDA analyzers writing DARs. Each should either route through app.db.save_and_sync (canonical pattern) or mirror Stage A's post-dbt-seed export+invalidate chain. Not addressed in this session.

title: Stage A blockers stored with resolves_in routing but not yet consumed by downstream EDA stages
description: Stage A's scope derivation emits structured blockers with 6 augmentation fields (short_title, what_it_means, what_llm_needs, resolves_in, resolves_via, user_action_now — landed 2026-04-22 augmentation). `resolves_in` is the routing taxonomy: domain_eda / term_eda / analyst_decision / ingestion_required. Currently stored in scope_derivation_history_json.iterations[].llm_response.blockers[] and displayed in the Term Scope tab UI with expander + badge. NOT yet consumed by Domain EDA or Term EDA prompts. The cross-stage contract is documented in the design. Fix direction: Stage B (Domain EDA refactor) reads blockers scoped to its target table with resolves_in='domain_eda' and ensures the relevant analyzer fires. Stage C (Term EDA) consumes all term blockers and emits per-blocker resolution status. Neither is built yet.
resolution: Stage B landed [commit: 4c1593c] — blocker consumption via scripts/_stage_a_blocker_loader.py; prompt injection in 4 LLM-backed DAR analyzers (completeness, dimensions, magnitude, code_tables); blockers_addressed field in result_json uniform across all 8 DAR analysis_types (the 4 non-LLM types — date, segmentation, grain_relationship, performance_baseline — emit []). Stage C landed [commit: 8df7716] — closes full cross-stage blocker consumption pipeline (A→B→C). Term EDA consumes resolves_in='term_eda' blockers via scripts/run_term_eda.py; four-status enum (resolved/escalated_to_analyst/could_not_resolve/not_applicable) with a two-predicate test on `resolved`; blockers_resolution recorded on the sufficiency row per the cross-stage contract. Full pipeline now operational.

title: Stage B observation emission deferred
description: Stage B v1 (landed 2026-04-22) consumes Stage A blockers only. The design explicitly permits Stage B to emit its own observations as DAR findings with analysis_type='observation' if the LLM discovers new concerns during analysis, but Stage B v1 does not implement this path. The blockers_addressed field already carries per-blocker resolution status; observation emission would be additive on top.
resolution: Re-open when an actual pattern of useful Stage B observations is identified. Until then, analysts who want to surface new concerns can author them as Stage A blockers via Term Scope revise + confirm. Future implementation: extend run_*_analysis.py to emit observation DARs with analysis_type='observation' alongside the existing analysis_type rows, and update Term Scope tab to render those alongside Stage A blockers.

title: Term Scope tab does not render Stage B per-blocker status
description: Stage B (landed 2026-04-22) writes blockers_addressed into DAR result_json. The data is queryable but the Term Scope tab does not yet render per-blocker status across stages — the design's visibility invariant is only partially satisfied (Stage A blockers visible; Stage B addressing not surfaced; Stage C resolution not yet built). Connective tissue between 'Stage A flagged X' and 'Stage B addressed it as Y with evidence Z' lives only in raw DAR JSON.
resolution: Defer to a single UX pass once Stage C lands so the three-stage view can be built coherently. Until then: analysts can inspect blockers_addressed via the Per-Table Domain EDA section's 'blockers_addressed count' column on the analyzer status grid + click into the relevant DAR row in the existing DAR-browsing surfaces. Implementation when ready: extend Term Scope tab's blocker expander to show per-blocker addressing status from latest DAR per (term, table) + Stage C resolution status when available.

title: check_prerequisites next_steps omits tables with any existing DAR regardless of term context
description: check_prerequisites filters scope tables to those needing Domain EDA by querying domain_analysis_results WHERE source_tables=<table> AND status='success'. This does not scope by term context — any existing success DAR for a table marks it "done" in next_steps even if the DAR predates the current term's confirmation. Observed during BG027 smoke test (2026-04-22): mseg and mkpf had pre-existing success DARs from 2026-04-19 (term-analysis dev work), so both were omitted from next_steps despite being in BG027's scope without BG027-context Domain EDA. Cosmetic — Domain tab reads blockers per-table directly, not via next_steps. May need term-context filtering or a "needs refresh" signal.

title: DAR supersede behavior on same-(table analysis_type) re-runs not verified downstream
description: Stage B smoke test produced a new code_tables DAR (DAR-00058) for mseg when a prior successful code_tables DAR for mseg existed (2026-04-19). Did not observe whether the prior DAR was marked status='superseded' automatically or whether both rows now coexist as status='success'. Downstream consumers (Layer A compile, check_prerequisites, Term Scope tab's "blockers_addressed count" column) rely on DAR status+recency conventions. Verify: (a) supersede-by pointer is written on the older row on re-run; (b) downstream consumers filter to latest-success-per-(table, type) correctly. Pre-existing behavior — not introduced by Stage B, but Stage B's UI makes re-run pattern easier to trigger.

title: Stage B blockers_addressed status picks generous 'addressed' when sibling-DAR context satisfies blocker
description: Two BG027 smoke-test datapoints (DAR-00058 code_tables, DAR-00059 completeness) both produced status='addressed' for the BWART semantics blocker, but only DAR-00058 directly surfaced BWART distributions. DAR-00059 (completeness) cannot surface distinct values at all — its SQL produces null counts only. Yet its evidence text referenced "prior domain analysis" (DAR-00058 visible in context bundle) and picked 'addressed'. Pattern: LLM interprets the two-predicate test's "analysis you ran on THIS table" broadly, including sibling DARs in context, rather than strictly "output from THIS analyzer's SQL." Impact: Stage C routing loses signal on which blockers each analyzer individually addressed. Fix direction for the next prompt iteration: sharpen the predicate wording in the prompt to "output from THIS analyzer's SQL" with explicit directive that sibling DARs are context, not evidence. No code change needed — prompt-text-only tuning. Accumulate more datapoints before iterating.

title: Legacy UI removed in Stage D.1 — data preserved for audit
description: Stage D.1 removed the legacy domain_facts UI from the Domain Analysis tab (~354 LOC) and the legacy analysis_findings per-query exploration UI from the Business Term Analysis tab (~608 LOC). Underlying seed data (`dbt/seeds/domain_facts.csv`, `dbt/seeds/analysis_findings.csv`) remains in place for historical audit. schema.yml entries + `_context_assembler` loaders (business layer for domain_facts; dynamic layer for analysis_findings) preserved — term-analysis context bundles still reference these seeds where applicable. New writes go through Stage B per-table analyzer dispatch (DARs) and Stage C Term EDA (TARs). If analysts need to query historical legacy data, use direct DuckDB queries.
resolution: UI removed in commit 80b1879. Seeds retained. Shared helpers (`rewrite_sql_for_staging`, `run_query_with_fallback`, `load_actual_staging_schema`) extracted to `app/_data_analysis_shared.py` so tab_explore and tab_report continue working post-deletion.

title: S2T tab hard-wired to legacy analysis_findings gate
description: render_ask_claude in Business_Glossary.py required analysis_findings rows before rendering the Create S2T button (lines 1121-1139 of pre-Stage-D.2 code). Terms processed through the Stage A→B→C pipeline never produce analysis_findings rows and thus could not access Create S2T via the UI, even after reaching status='ready_for_s2t'. Blocked end-to-end analyst workflow; forced manual Python dispatch for staged-pipeline terms.
resolution: Stage D.2 refactored render_ask_claude with dual-path gate: legacy (analysis_findings rows exist) OR new-pipeline (status IN ('ready_for_s2t', 'approved')). Both paths now reach the Create S2T button. Pipeline-state awareness added to tab_spec: status badge + pipeline progress strip + action panel with deep-links to Data Analysis tabs. Helpers extracted to `app/_s2t_tab_helpers.py`. Eligibility logic extracted to pure `is_s2t_eligible` function with dedicated unit tests. Commit 9c29be8.

title: Re-run S2T flow — deferred from Stage D.2
description: Stage D.2 originally specified a Re-run S2T button for approved+non-empty terms. Dropped because re-running collides with deployed .sql files and existing s2t_mapping rows — `create_s2t_with_implementation` has no archival semantics. Safe re-run requires (a) renaming existing `dbt/models/<layer>/<target_model>.sql` into `archive/<ARC-ID>/`, (b) superseding s2t_mapping rows for the term before regeneration, (c) a rollback path if the new run fails. Too large for Stage D.2.
resolution: Workaround: analyst can deny the approved S2T (via tab_detail approval form) to preserve audit trail, then re-run via legacy path if analysis_findings exists; OR archive the entire term and recreate. Proper fix: separate follow-up commit specifying archival policy for in-place S2T re-runs. Scope: design doc + runner changes + new rollback tests.

title: business_glossary.status='denied' missing from accepted_values
description: render_approval_form in Business_Glossary.py writes status='denied' when an analyst denies a term (line 1079 of pre-Stage-D.2 code). But schema.yml's accepted_values enumeration for business_glossary.status did NOT include 'denied'. Any successful denial would have failed `dbt test` on the next seed cycle. Pre-existing gap, not introduced by Stage D.2.
resolution: Stage D.2 added 'denied' to schema.yml:172 accepted_values for business_glossary.status. Also wired denied-status handling in tab_spec action panel (renders notes_excerpt from business_glossary.notes as best-effort denial context since no structured denial_reason column exists). Commit 9c29be8.

title: Stage D.2 gating used term_s2t.empty — misrouted Stage A-only mappings
description: Stage D.2 tab_spec branched on `term_s2t.empty` and passed the same flag to `get_s2t_action` as `term_s2t_empty`. But Stage A's `_scope_derivation._rewrite_s2t_for_term` writes s2t_mapping rows at scope_confirmed time with `target_model=NULL` (source-only rows carrying scope + rationale, no target mapping yet). For a term like BG027 at status='ready_for_s2t' with 5 Stage A rows, `term_s2t.empty` was False so tab_spec routed into the existing-S2T lineage/SQL/samples branch — the "🤖 Create S2T with Claude" button never rendered. Broken for every staged-pipeline term at ready_for_s2t until Deploy had populated target_model.
resolution: Introduced `has_piece8_s2t_rows(term_s2t_df)` discriminator in `app/_s2t_tab_helpers.py` — returns True iff at least one row has populated `target_model`. Renamed `get_s2t_action` parameter from `term_s2t_empty` to `has_piece8_mapping` (inverted semantics) with docstring explaining the Stage A / Deploy distinction. tab_spec branch + caller updated. 3 new unit tests for the helper (empty df, Stage A only, mixed Stage A + Deploy row). 4 existing get_s2t_action tests renamed/flipped to match new semantics (ready_for_s2t / approved × with/without mapping). Commit b51eac9.

title: Scope re-derivation may delete Deploy-produced s2t_mapping rows
description: `_scope_derivation._rewrite_s2t_for_term` at scripts/_scope_derivation.py:752 deletes ALL prior s2t_mapping rows for the term before inserting Stage A rows. If an analyst re-runs scope derivation on a term that has already been through Create S2T + Deploy, the Deploy-produced rows (target_model + transformation_logic_plain + join/filter descriptions) are silently destroyed. No warning, no archival. Latent hazard: UI currently allows scope re-derivation on any term regardless of whether Deploy has landed.
resolution: Added _has_deployed_s2t_rows(term_id) helper + guard in confirm_scope (scripts/_scope_derivation.py) that refuses re-derivation when any s2t_mapping row for the term has target_model populated. Returns ConfirmationResult(success=False) with actionable error text pointing to Direction C (Re-run S2T flow) and the workaround (create new term). Narrow hotfix: no primitive integration, no cascade, no successor handling — those ship in Direction C. 3 new unit tests in tests/test_scope_derivation.py validate happy/blocked/unknown-term paths. Single-file change + test file (verified UI caller at Data_Analysis.py:1144 already renders ConfirmationResult.error via st.error). Commit 7509c6e.

title: s2t_mapping undocumented in schema.yml
description: schema.yml has no entry for s2t_mapping. The 14-column shape (id, business_term_id, business_term_name, source_table, source_field, source_description, target_model, target_column, transformation_logic_plain, transformation_logic_sql, join_description, filter_description, notes, transformation_logic_plain_business) is derived from CSV header + DuckDB introspection only.
