No not_null tests, no accepted_values for target_model layer prefixes, no relationships test to business_glossary.id. dbt tests cannot catch schema drift or orphan rows.

title: Deploy appends to s2t_mapping without dedup vs Stage A rows
description: Create S2T + Deploy at Business_Glossary.py:1448 opens s2t_mapping.csv in `'a'` (append) mode and writes per-source-table rows with populated target_model. Stage A rows for the same term (target_model=NULL, notes='stage_a_derived') are not deleted or superseded. Both coexist indefinitely. Existing-S2T rendering in tab_spec mostly dropna's target_model so the duplicate rows are hidden from Target, S2T Full SQL, and Sample Data panels. But the Source Tables panel (Business_Glossary.py:2305-2311) iterates `term_s2t[['source_table', 'source_field', 'source_description']].drop_duplicates()` — if Stage A and Deploy wrote the same (source_table, source_field), they collapse fine; if they diverged on source_description text, the panel shows both. Cosmetic only, no downstream data-quality impact.

title: Source characterization happens post-scope — inverting the natural pipeline architecture
description: Stage A scope derivation consumed only LLM-recalled SAP knowledge + sap_data_dictionary field-level rows. Rich per-table `semantic_model` catalog (20-column schema) was populated for 1 of 42 raw_sap tables. Relational structure had no authoritative source — information_schema lacks FK constraints (0 of 42 tables declare PK/FK via DuckDB constraints), no SAP DD tables ingested. Stage A proposed tables based on the LLM's trained SAP knowledge without data-grounded verification. Source understanding happened per-term via Domain EDA, never globally.
resolution: Stage F landed across 4 commits: (1) analyzer registry + schema.yml patch, (2) run_schema_discovery_analysis.py (PK/FK/shape/bridge analyzer with sum-match, role='key' pruning, bridges_only refresh mode), (3) Data Catalog Source Diagnostic UI (Views A/B/C with bulk dispatch + Compile Semantic Model button), (4) Stage A prompt rewrite + tiered evidence rendering + `_merge_schema_discovery_fks` deterministic post-pass + new blocker category `resolves_in='source_diagnostic_required'` + post-demo roadmap. Commit 7273381.

title: Post-demo work — term-analysis attestation completeness + architectural hygiene
description: Series of gaps documented in the post-demo roadmap. Includes: TAR-NNNNN citation format not taught to the term-analysis runner; no `tar_consumed` attestation field; deterministic-analyzer directives missing (date / segmentation / grain_relationship / performance_baseline / schema_discovery all load but have no consumption instructions); skipped-DAR semantics not rendered; Stage A blocker surfacing absent (scope_derivation_history_json entirely invisible to the runner). Also covers the Re-run S2T archival flow, SAP DD ingestion, analyst-authored catalog enrichment UI, Business Term Analysis tab enhancements, Data Model reconciliation, and the grain_relationship deprecation decision.
resolution: Multiple commits post-BG027 demo, prioritized by demo observations.

title: Grain_relationship analyzer discovery function subsumed by schema_discovery
description: schema_discovery (Stage F) includes sum-match evidence in its relationship_shapes output for 1:N header-detail classifications, fully subsuming grain_relationship's discovery function. Grain_relationship remains potentially useful for term-scoped verification (verify relationships hold under term's data filters), but this is speculative without evidence of term-specific divergence.
resolution: Evaluate post-demo. If term-scoped verification adds no signal, remove grain_relationship analyzer entirely. If it does, relabel its section in Business Term Analysis tab to 'Term-Scoped Relationship Verification' and make it optional (not a Stage C prereq).

title: Archive term flow doesn't handle downstream dbt model dependencies
description: app/archive_term.py moves a mart model's .sql file to dbt/models/archive/<ARC-ID>/ without updating or disabling the downstream dbt models that depend on it. dbt compile then fails because downstream models reference a disabled node, blocking dbt seed as a side effect. Observed during pre-demo attempt to archive BG011 (total_purchase_orders, fact_purchase_orders mart). 4 downstream models affected: knowledge_procurement_health, knowledge_vendor_performance, obt_procurement_overview, obt_vendor_scorecard. Secondary observation: archive UI lacks loading/error feedback, causing user to click archive twice. archive_log gained two rows (ARC-20260423-001 and -002) but only -001's file moves executed; business_glossary pointed to -002 leaving a pointer/file mismatch. Full diagnostic trace preserved in commit message. Same category as #65 (scope re-derivation deletes Deploy rows): pipeline operations don't understand their own output graph.
resolution: Strict-cascade saga in app/archive_term.py + dependency analyzer in app/archive_dependency_analyzer.py + guided-unwind UI in app/_archive_ui.py. Block-if-shared policy chosen (Q2 confirmed). Three blocker classes: sharing (s2t_mapping ownership) / downstream (manifest reverse-refs) / in-flight (re-entry guard). 36 unit tests + BG011 live-data smoke + dbt parse/seed verified. New archive_log columns: cascaded_models_json + blockers_resolved. LLM-context filter audit (decision #64) confirmed clean across 11 BG-reading files. See decisions #110 (implementation) and #111 (meta-rule).

title: DAR supersede not firing across 6 of 7 analyzers; stale/skipped DARs win latest-by-timestamp in term-analysis context bundles
description: domain_analysis_results has superseded_by column present but unpopulated by 6 of 7 Stage B/F analyzers (completeness, dimensions, magnitude, code_tables, temporal_coverage, segmentation_threshold). Only schema_discovery implements supersede, and only in --mode bridges_only path (View C Pass 2). All other analyzer re-runs append DAR rows with superseded_by='' without marking prior rows. The term-analysis context assembler's _load_dynamic reads DARs via ORDER BY executed_at_utc DESC LIMIT 50 with no superseded_by filter. When re-runs occur, multiple current-flagged DARs for the same (analysis_type, source_tables) pair reach the LLM. Worst case observed: EQUI magnitude — first run succeeded (DAR-00062), second run skipped (DAR-00081 status=skipped). Latest-by-timestamp picks the skip DAR, so the term-analysis bundle sees no magnitude evidence for EQUI despite a successful analysis in seed. Discovered during BG027 Create S2T prep after user ran EQUI through Source Diagnostic twice.
resolution: Fixed by commit c8a16e3 (shared scripts/_dar_supersede.py helper wired into all 7 analyzers; scripts/_context_assembler.py filters superseded_by IS NULL) and commit d3e7030 (scripts/backfill_dar_supersede.py executed to flip 41 duplicate-current pre-fix DARs to superseded across all affected tables). Post-backfill verification: no (analysis_type, source_tables) pair has >1 current DAR except legitimate same-invocation multi-row analyzers (temporal_coverage, segmentation_threshold, performance_baseline, grain_relationship) where all current rows share identical executed_at_utc from the latest run.

title: Term-analysis context assembler drops dbt_column_lineage rows in raw_sap.<table> origin_table form
description: scripts/_context_assembler.py _load_ontology filters dbt_column_lineage.origin_table via WHERE LOWER(origin_table) IN (scope), where scope is bare lowercase table names from resolve_scope (e.g., ['equi', 'mseg']). But dbt_column_lineage stores origin_table in the dominant 'raw_sap.<table>' form for raw sources (verified: 100% of rows for BG027 scope use the raw_sap.<t> form, zero use the bare form). Result: all ontology rows silently dropped from the term-analysis context bundle, breaking the consumer-priority design which says 'ontology first, Layer A second' for ontology-covered tables. Discovered 2026-04-24 during BG027 Create S2T prep — 57 matching rows across 5 scope tables (equi/mseg/mkpf/objk/mard) were missing from bundle despite being present in the seed. compile_semantic_model.has_ontology_coverage (scripts/compile_semantic_model.py:152-157) handles this correctly with a triple-pattern (bare, raw_sap.<t>, any.<t>); the ontology loader used only the bare pattern.
resolution: Fixed by commit 5694d79. Expanded WHERE clause in _load_ontology to the same triple-pattern used by has_ontology_coverage (bare OR raw_sap.<t> OR any.<t>). Unit test added to tests/test_context_assembler.py asserts the query returns rows when origin_table is stored as raw_sap.<t> and scope passes bare <t>.

title: LLM source diagnostic analyzers crash on fresh tables with zero prior DARs; batch dispatch swallows crashes silently
description: The 4 LLM-backed Source Diagnostic analyzers (completeness, dimensions, magnitude, code_tables) call scripts/_context_assembler.assemble_context with strict=True. When invoked on a raw table with zero prior DARs, the HEAVY 'dynamic' layer is empty and ContextDegradedError is raised. The exception is uncaught by the analyzer's try/except (which only catches ContextScopeError), so the Python process exits with an unhandled traceback. Data_Catalog.py's _run_source_diagnostic_on_all (View C batch dispatch) only counts aggregate returncode==0 results; per-analyzer stderr is captured via capture_output=True but never surfaced in the UI. User sees 'N✓/M✗' in the status box with no per-failure detail. Discovered during BG027 Create S2T prep: user's batch run on 5 scope tables succeeded for equi/mseg/mkpf (which had prior DARs from earlier sessions) but silently failed for the 4 LLM analyzers on objk/mard (both fresh tables). logs/assemble_context.jsonl contains 8 ContextDegradedError entries during the batch window 01:07-01:08 on 2026-04-24, matching the 4 LLM analyzers × 2 fresh tables pattern.
resolution: Two-commit fix: (1) Commit e36a924 — LLM analyzers call new scripts/_context_assembler.has_prior_dars_for_scope helper and pass strict=False when the scope has zero prior DARs. Degraded bundle still populates from Layer A/B + ontology signals (known_issue #74 fix ensured ontology populates for ontology-covered scopes). (2) Commit d1d872d — app/pages/Data_Catalog.py:_run_source_diagnostic_on_all surfaces per-analyzer stderr via st.expander when any error is detected during a batch run. Headline aggregate counts preserved.

title: segmentation_threshold analyzer skips silently when all numeric columns are constant (stddev=0)
description: scripts/run_segmentation_analysis.py's per-column check skips constant numeric columns (MIN==MAX or stddev==0) via `continue`. When ALL numeric columns on a table are individually skipped, the outer loop completes with 0 emitted DARs and no row is written. The top-of-function skipped-DAR emit path (line 198-213) only fires when `cols` is empty from the start (no numeric columns at all). Result: a table that has numeric columns but all are constant produces no DAR — indistinguishable from 'analyzer hasn't run' in downstream prereq checks. Discovered 2026-04-24 during BG027 Create S2T prep: raw_sap.objk has one numeric column (OBZAE BIGINT), all rows have value=1, stddev=0.0. Segmentation's per-column check skips OBZAE and the outer loop emits nothing. The standard skipped-DAR emission pattern is violated in this edge case — the analyzer ran but didn't surface a status='skipped' row to carry the 'no meaningful thresholds' signal.
resolution: Fixed by commit 908c2ac. scripts/run_segmentation_analysis.py analyze_table gains an end-of-loop guard: when emitted == 0 and cols was non-empty (every numeric column individually skipped for being constant or all-null), emit a status="skipped" DAR with skip_reason="all N numeric column(s) constant or all-null". Matches the standard skipped-DAR emission pattern so term_eda_prereq and the context assembler see the analyzer as having run. Three regression tests in tests/test_segmentation_analysis.py cover: all-constant case emits skipped; mixed case (one column with findings) preserves success; no-numeric-columns case still emits skipped (pre-existing path preserved). Verified in production by running  post-fix — new skipped DAR for raw_sap.objk replaces the #76 gap in BG027 prereq coverage.

title: Term EDA prereq queries analysis_type='date' / 'segmentation' but DARs use 'temporal_coverage' / 'segmentation_threshold'
description: scripts/_term_eda_prereq._PER_TABLE_ANALYZERS uses UI-facing analyzer labels 'date' and 'segmentation' and queries main_seeds.domain_analysis_results with WHERE analysis_type = ? against those literal strings. But run_date_analysis.py writes DARs with analysis_type='temporal_coverage' (constant _ANALYSIS_TYPE at scripts/run_date_analysis.py:56) and run_segmentation_analysis.py writes analysis_type='segmentation_threshold' (constant at scripts/run_segmentation_analysis.py:54). Result: the prereq's SQL returns 0 rows for those two analyzers on EVERY scope table, reporting them as missing_analyzers_per_table even when temporal_coverage + segmentation_threshold DARs exist in the seed. Stage C is therefore unreachable for any term whose scope depends on production DARs with real storage labels. Discovered 2026-04-24 during BG027 Create S2T prep when the prereq check returned ready=False with missing_analyzers_per_table[t] = ['date', 'segmentation', ...] for all 5 scope tables despite full coverage. Why no prior regression signal: tests/test_term_eda_prereq.py's ALL_PER_TABLE fixture list uses the same UI labels as the buggy code ('date', 'segmentation'), so test fixtures insert DAR rows with analysis_type='date' / 'segmentation' — internally consistent with the buggy code path but never exercised against production DAR labels.
resolution: Fixed by commit ce24e8e. Introduced scripts/_term_eda_prereq._DAR_STORAGE_LABEL mapping {'date': 'temporal_coverage', 'segmentation': 'segmentation_threshold'}. Per-table analyzer loop now translates the UI label to the DAR storage label before the SQL query, preserving the user-facing output (next_steps still reads 'Run date, segmentation on <t>...' matching the Domain Analysis tab UI). Test fixture ALL_PER_TABLE updated to use production storage labels so test inserts align with what analyzers actually write. New regression test test_prereq_recognizes_temporal_coverage_and_segmentation_threshold_under_their_real_labels asserts ready=True when DARs carry production labels.

title: Compile Semantic Model UX: toast/panel contradiction on ontology-covered tables
description: Data Catalog → Source Diagnostic tab → Table Detail's Compile Semantic Model flow produced misleading UX on ontology-covered tables. Clicking Compile on e.g. EQUI returned rc=0 and the toast read 'Semantic model compiled for `equi`' — but compile_semantic_model.py skipped EQUI by design (consumer-priority discipline: ontology layer covers it via dbt_column_lineage's 13 matches for raw_sap.equi). No row is written to semantic_model.csv in the skip path. Below the toast, the Layer A Semantic Model panel rendered the empty state '⏳ Semantic model not yet compiled for this table. Run Source Diagnostic + Compile Semantic Model to populate.' — contradicting the success toast and instructing the user to run a compile that would skip again. Two UX defects, one root cause (skip-by-design path not distinguished from actual-compile / not-yet-compiled states): (a) toast lies — rc=0 covers both write and skip paths; (b) panel empty state conflates 'no DAR yet' / 'DAR but not compiled' / 'skipped by ontology' into one message. Discovered 2026-04-24 during BG027 Create S2T prep when the user ran Compile Semantic Model on EQUI and observed the contradiction. Data issue is NOT a bug — EQUI legitimately has ontology coverage; the skip is correct behavior. Only the reporting layer misleads.
resolution: Fixed by commit e09b1a4. Two pure helpers added to app/_data_catalog_helpers.py: (1) ontology_covers_table(conn, table) mirrors scripts/compile_semantic_model.has_ontology_coverage (triple-pattern match on dbt_column_lineage's origin_table); (2) parse_compile_skip_reason(stdout, table) extracts the 'skip <table> — <reason>' line emitted by compile_semantic_model.py:651/656/663. Compile handler in Data_Catalog.py re-queries main_seeds.semantic_model post-subprocess; if a row was written, shows the Compiled toast; otherwise parses stdout skip reason and surfaces an honest 'Skipped: <reason>' info message. Panel branch splits into three states: ontology-covered + no row → 'Layer A skipped by design, no action required' message citing the consumer-priority rule; not covered + no row → existing 'run compile' empty state; row exists → existing renderer. Tests: test_panel_branches_on_ontology_coverage + test_compile_toast_distinguishes_write_vs_skip in tests/test_data_catalog.py exercise both helpers across the three panel states and the write-vs-skip distinction.

title: compile_semantic_model skip logic conflates Layer A (LLM narrative) with dbt ontology (structural lineage)
description: compile_semantic_model.py's skip logic conflated two orthogonal context dimensions: Layer A (LLM-synthesized table-level semantic narrative — canonical_alias, typical_filters, common_traps, reference_sql, typical_use_cases) and the dbt ontology layer (dbt_column_lineage — auto-generated column-level structural lineage mapping raw_sap.<t> → staging). The consumer-priority discipline treated 'ontology coverage exists' as sufficient justification to skip Layer A compilation — but empirically the two layers provide non-overlapping context. Investigated 2026-04-24 during BG027 Create S2T prep. EQUI is ontology-covered (13 rows in dbt_column_lineage, all staging-layer, mostly empty descriptions, structural only). A Layer A row would add typical_filters/common_traps/reference_sql/canonical_alias/temporal_coverage_json/typical_values_range_json/natural_thresholds_json — none of which the ontology carries. For BG027's 5 ontology-covered scope tables, the bundle currently carries column-level descriptions (sap_data_dictionary) + DAR evidence + s2t_mapping examples, but no pre-digested table-level narrative. The LLM re-synthesizes reasoning it could read off a Layer A row. The term-analysis context assembler loads static (Layer A) and ontology layers in parallel for create_s2t + pre_s2t_reasoning purposes — verified via LAYER_LOADERS dispatch at _context_assembler.py:1473. No cross-layer suppression; the data gap is purely upstream in compile_semantic_model's skip gate.
resolution: Resolves by commit 147b81a. Refactor: remove the has_ontology_coverage skip gate from compile_semantic_model.py main() (only skip condition removed; preserved + DAR-incomplete gates retained as legitimate domain guards). Existing merge logic (final.update(preserved) at line 686) continues to idempotently overlay human-protected rows on top of fresh synthesis and refresh auto_generated rows on recompile. Data_Catalog.py Layer A panel collapses from 3-way (row-exists / ontology-covered / uncovered) to 2-way (row-exists / run-compile prompt). app/_data_catalog_helpers.ontology_covers_table retired (no remaining caller); has_semantic_model_row added. Three new tests assert: compile no longer references has_ontology_coverage; auto_generated rows remain refreshable on recompile; context_assembler bundles both Layer A and ontology for same table when both have rows. The corresponding post-demo roadmap bullet marked resolved inline; Layer B staging-only filter bullet remains open (separate issue).

title: compile_semantic_model silently degrades to empty-bundle mode on DuckDB connection contention, producing LLM-hallucinated narratives
description: compile_semantic_model.py silently degrades to empty-bundle mode when assemble_context fails to open its DuckDB connection — usually because Streamlit is running and holding a read-only connection with different configuration. The error surfaces as `WARNING: bundle assembly failed, proceeding with empty bundle: Connection Error: Can't open a connection to same database file with a different configuration than existing connections` printed to stdout only. LLM synthesis proceeds with an empty DAR bundle, so typical_filters / common_traps / typical_values_range_json / natural_thresholds_json / temporal_coverage_json are drawn from LLM general knowledge of SAP table conventions instead of being grounded in actual DAR payloads. The written row has review_state='auto_generated' — no flag distinguishing it from a grounded-compile row. Analyst sees authoritative-looking narrative that may be fabricated. Concrete evidence discovered 2026-04-24 during BG027 Create S2T prep: first compile on raw_sap.equi (streamlit running) produced common_traps='INBDT has 40% null values for startup dates' — actual DAR shows INBDT null_pct=0.0 across 45000 rows (DAR-00114 completeness). The 40% figure was LLM hallucination from SAP training-data patterns, not from project-specific evidence. Severity: high. Degraded rows look correct but are less useful than they appear. Compile UI/toast gives no indication the bundle was empty — the rc=0 return + 'Wrote N rows' message reads as successful.
resolution: Fixed by commit 608b941 (conn threading) + d5f9cfb (data: recompile 6 tables with grounded bundles). Root cause was NOT Streamlit contention as initially suspected — it was in-process: compile_semantic_model.main() opens a read-write DuckDB conn at line 719 for the dbt-seed subprocess, then _assemble_bundle called assemble_context() without passing that conn, so assemble_context opened a second read-only conn which conflicts with the read-write one (DuckDB forbids multiple configs per process per DB). assemble_context raised, _assemble_bundle swallowed, LLM synthesized narratives from SAP priors. Fix: thread conn through _assemble_bundle(..., conn=conn) into assemble_context(..., conn=conn) which already supported the optional-conn param (added in an earlier bugfix, never consumed). Regression test at tests/test_compile_semantic_model_merge.py::test_assemble_bundle_accepts_conn_and_avoids_empty_bundle_warning captures stdout + asserts no degradation warning. Verified production impact: EQUI pre-fix reference_sql used WHERE EQART='CPE' (SAP generic); post-fix uses WHERE HERST='Huawei Technologies' (real manufacturer from dimensions DAR top-N). Five BG027 scope tables recompiled; zmm_approval_log backfilled; zphase2_fixture left as-is (dev fixture, DAR-incomplete, not demo-blocker).

title: Subprocess dbt seed reseed fails silently when parent Python conn holds cpe_analytics.duckdb — class bug across all sync_parquet_and_invalidate consumers
description: scripts/_parquet_sync.sync_parquet_and_invalidate reseeds DuckDB via a subprocess `dbt seed --select <seed>` call. On Windows, that subprocess fails with 'IO Error: Cannot open file ...: The process cannot access the file because it is being used by another process' whenever the parent Python process has an open read-write connection to cpe_analytics.duckdb at the moment the subprocess launches — DuckDB's file lock is exclusive. The helper's except block catches the non-zero returncode, prints a WARNING to stderr, and returns early (no parquet export). Parent continues, returns rc=0 because the CSV-level write already succeeded. UI shows success; DuckDB view + parquet stay stale. Today's reproduction (2026-04-24 BG027 Term EDA): scripts/run_term_eda.py:824 calls _tar_writer.write_tar_run while the parent conn opened at line 547 is still held open. write_tar_run invokes sync_parquet_and_invalidate → dbt seed subprocess launches → blocked by parent's file lock → IO Error at 13:49:17 per dbt/logs/dbt.log → WARNING swallowed → CSV has 17 TAR rows for BG027 but DuckDB has 0. Second reseed (for business_glossary via _update_term_status at line 849) happens AFTER conn.close() at line 848, succeeds. Net: BG027.status flipped to ready_for_s2t but TAR evidence invisible in UI. Class bug: every consumer of sync_parquet_and_invalidate is implicitly required to close their cpe_analytics.duckdb conn before the helper runs, without programmatic enforcement. compile_semantic_model.py does this correctly (line 728-729 with inline comment); run_term_eda.py does it for _update_term_status but forgets for write_tar_run; _scope_derivation.py opens read-only which also holds a shared lock blocking subprocess RW. Streamlit is NOT the culprit — app/db.py docstring confirms Streamlit uses `duckdb.connect(':memory:')` + parquet views, never touches cpe_analytics.duckdb. Verified by reproducing the warning with Streamlit stopped.
resolution: Resolved by 6-commit sequence: eab30e6 (file #81 open with diagnostic), 94ba7f4 (scripts/audit_csv_duckdb_parity.py + pre-fix baseline), 2b8788c (scripts/_parquet_sync.py Option A rewrite + 6 regression tests covering parent-conn-open path, schema.yml column_types enforcement, no-subprocess guarantee, parquet output, untyped-seed auto-inference with per-column type assertions), 6f0a88a (reconciliation: reseeded the 2 pre-fix divergent seeds via the new helper; all 35 seeds at PARITY; CSV data for today workflow committed), 3b832ed (file follow-up #82 for untyped-seed column_types in schema.yml), and this commit (hash backfill + flip to resolved). Verification: post-fix audit (logs/audit_post_81_fix.json) 35 PARITY / 0 DIVERGENT. BG027 TAR rows visible in DuckDB (17 rows) + status=ready_for_s2t in both CSV and DuckDB. Streamlit architecture confirmed correct and untouched — app/db.py uses duckdb.connect(":memory:") + parquet views per design.

title: Four critical seeds lack column_types in schema.yml — behavioral dependency on DuckDB read_csv_auto inference
description: Four critical seeds used by the term-analysis pipeline + operational flows lack explicit `config.column_types` in dbt/seeds/schema.yml: domain_analysis_results, business_glossary, s2t_mapping, known_issues. After the #81 Option A rewrite (commit 2b8788c), sync_parquet_and_invalidate falls back to DuckDB's read_csv_auto with sample_size=-1 (full-file scan) for untyped seeds. This is strictly at least as good as dbt seed's default partial sniff and handles today's data correctly — verified via commit 6f0a88a reconciliation where all 35 seeds converged to PARITY including the untyped four. However, untyped inference is a behavioral dependency on DuckDB itself. A future DuckDB version changing inference heuristics, or a future seed addition with ambiguous columns (e.g., an all-empty column that had been VARCHAR but is now INTEGER-sniffed, repeating the #73 class), would drift silently. Explicit column_types in schema.yml pin the contract at the project level, independent of DuckDB behavior. Discovered during #81 design review (2026-04-24).
resolution: Post-demo cleanup. Add `config: column_types:` blocks to dbt/seeds/schema.yml for the four untyped seeds: domain_analysis_results (already has partial coverage via individual column entries but no column_types block — convert to explicit types matching current read_csv_auto inference), business_glossary (id varchar, term_name varchar, status varchar with accepted_values, definition varchar, archive_id varchar, etc.), s2t_mapping (not yet in schema.yml at all — Direction C Commit 6a partially addresses per known_issue #66; this would complete that work), known_issues (id integer or bigint, title varchar, status varchar, priority varchar, created_date date, resolved_date date nullable). Scope estimate: ~120 LOC additions to schema.yml, zero runtime code changes. Tests: extend tests/test_parquet_sync.py::test_typed_seed_creates_column_types_from_schema_yml to cover each of the four newly-typed seeds. Not blocking — current untyped fallback works correctly. Priority: medium (foundational hardening, not a user-visible bug).

title: S2T deploy parser strips all 'S' chars, crashes on modern S2T-NNNN ID scheme
description: Create S2T deploy flow in app/pages/Business_Glossary.py:1446 uses an unanchored string-strip to parse the max existing s2t_mapping.id: `lambda x: int(x.replace('S', '')) if x.startswith('S') else 0`. replace('S', '') removes EVERY 'S' character, not just a leading prefix. Works for the legacy SNNN scheme (S037 → '037' → 37) but raises on any ID with 'S' anywhere after the first char. Modern scope-confirmation stubs use the S2T-NNNN scheme (e.g., S2T-0001 per scripts/_scope_derivation.py): 'S2T-0001'.replace('S', '') = '2T-0001' → int('2T-0001') → ValueError: invalid literal for int() with base 10: '2T-0001'. The deploy bails at Step a before writing; rollback is clean. Today's s2t_mapping.csv has 45 rows mixing three schemes: 33 legacy SNNN (S001-S045-ish), 7 term-prefixed BG028-NN, and 5 modern S2T-NNNN (BG027's stubs). Deploying any term whose scope-stub IDs follow the modern scheme trips the parser on first read. Secondary issue: the row-writer at line 1455 formats new IDs with the legacy `S{N:03d}` template. Even after a parser fix, newly-written rows would add to the legacy scheme rather than align with scope_derivation's S2T-NNNN stubs, perpetuating the mixed-scheme drift. Discovered 2026-04-24 during BG027 first deploy attempt. deploy_debug.log 15:39:55 captures the exact ValueError + clean rollback.
resolution: Fixed by commit e91bd7d. (1) New helper app/_s2t_tab_helpers.extract_trailing_digits(x) uses regex (\d+)$ to pull trailing digits from any ID regardless of prefix: SNNN, S2T-NNNN, BG028-NN, or any other scheme. Returns 0 for IDs without trailing digits. (2) Business_Glossary.py:1444 replaces the buggy lambda with the helper, resolving the ValueError. (3) Row-writer at line 1455 updated to format new IDs as `S2T-{N:04d}` to align with scope_derivation's stub scheme. Max-based N prevents collision with any existing ID regardless of scheme (new IDs always numerically exceed the global max). (4) Regression test tests/test_s2t_tab_helpers.py covers: mixed-scheme CSV (SNNN + S2T-NNNN + BG028-NN) → parser picks correct global max; new IDs generated as S2T-NNNN and don't collide with any existing ID.

title: Deploy auto-retry classifier only handles Binder column-not-found; broader SQL failures (IO spill, syntax, type mismatch) skip retry and roll back unrecoverably
description: Create S2T deploy's auto-retry mechanism (introduced 2026-04-18 in commit 41a78f7) is scoped narrow to DuckDB Binder column-not-found errors only. Classifier pattern: `Referenced column "X" not found`. Any failure outside that — IO errors, Parser/syntax errors, Catalog/table-not-found, type-conversion errors, memory spills — falls through `_log('error not a parseable Binder error — skipping retry')` at Business_Glossary.py:1655, breaks out of the retry loop, and rolls back immediately. The user must restart the entire deploy (Create S2T → review → click Deploy again) to try a different SQL. Surfaced 2026-04-24 by BG027's deploy attempt. LLM-generated SQL joined equi×mseg on shared MATNR (material-master ID), producing a ~300M+ intermediate-row cartesian. DuckDB spilled, failed with IO Error on a Windows temp-directory path. Classifier saw no Binder signature, declined retry, rolled back. Deploy is one-shot for any non-Binder failure mode. Repair function app/claude_api.repair_dbt_model_sql accepts free-form dbt_error_text already; the gate preventing it from being called on non-Binder errors is entirely in the classifier. Prompt could take a class-specific hint to steer repair (e.g., 'IO spill → examine JOIN cardinality' vs 'Binder column → pick from candidates') but currently doesn't.
resolution: Option B — classifier widening + error-class hint mapping. Fixed by commit bb67e3a. (1) New helper app/_s2t_tab_helpers.classify_dbt_error(err_text) returns dict with should_retry, hint, failed_col, candidates, failed_model. Covers 8 classes: timeout (no retry), empty (generic retry), Binder column-not-found (narrow), Catalog/table-not-found, Parser/syntax, Type/conversion, IO/spill/OOM, unknown catch-all. (2) app/claude_api.repair_dbt_model_sql accepts optional error_hint parameter — injected into user prompt before the raw error text. System prompt widened to describe all repair classes (not Binder-only). (3) Business_Glossary.py Step d replaces _parse_binder_error + `if not failed_col: break` with classify_dbt_error + `if not should_retry: break`. Hint threaded through to repair call. (4) Tests: 8 tests in test_s2t_tab_helpers.py — one per error class — assert should_retry, hint presence, and extraction of failed_col where applicable. Not a full fix for BG027 specifically: the MATNR-cartesian SQL may or may not be repaired by the LLM even with the cardinality hint, because the repair call lacks empirical cardinality evidence (grain_relationship analyzer's 'skipped — no shared numeric columns' — to be addressed separately in known_issue #85). This fix unblocks retry for the failure CLASS; #85 addresses the content of the repair evidence.

title: Validator cardinality coverage gap on bridge-intermediary tables
description: The S2T cardinality validator gates only on cardinality DARs that exist for the pair in payload['join_path']. When propose_scope's analyzer scope omits a table that the LLM uses as a bridge intermediary or terminus, that pair has no DARs and the validator silently passes. Surfaced in Step C BG027 verification (2026-04-25): the join_cardinality analyzer ran on --tables equi,mseg,mkpf,objk,mard, so pairs objk<->seri and seri<->mseg have no DARs. The LLM proposed a SERI-bridged join_path and the validator could not gate on those legs (it correctly didn't, since no evidence existed). LLM choice happened to be empirically correct (SERI bridge is per_record_key per DAR-00328 measured during the equi<->mseg pair analysis), so no harm. But a hostile or mistaken LLM proposing a bad join through a bridge-only intermediary would not be caught. Severity: low — direct enumeration covers same-pair candidates and the LLM tends to choose evidence-backed paths. The validator work is functionally complete; this is forward hardening.
resolution: Closed by the lazy-analysis follow-up commit. scripts/_s2t_cardinality_validator.trigger_lazy_analysis invokes scripts/run_join_cardinality_analysis.analyze_pair(conn, t1, t2, all_tables) when validate_s2t_sql encounters a join whose pair has no cardinality DAR. After analyze_pair appends to dbt/seeds/domain_analysis_results.csv, the validator refreshes the in-DB main_seeds.domain_analysis_results table from the CSV and re-looks up — so the just-emitted DAR is queryable in the same call. Verified by BG027 e2e: validator hit objk<->seri and seri<->mseg pairs without DARs, triggered lazy analysis (~30-60s per pair), captured the new DARs, and the SQL passed validation. Cost is the lazy-analysis latency the issue called out; closes the gap that #92 named. Tests: tests/test_s2t_cardinality_validator.py covers test_lazy_analysis_triggered_on_missing_dar (call site fires) and test_lazy_analysis_persists_dar_then_revalidates (post-trigger re-lookup catches the new DAR).

title: Term-analysis DAR loader LIMIT 50 saturated by recent join_cardinality DARs - EDA-analyzer DARs hidden from production bundles
description: scripts/_context_assembler.py:_load_dynamic pulls non-superseded DARs via SELECT ... FROM main_seeds.domain_analysis_results WHERE superseded_by IS NULL OR superseded_by = '' ORDER BY executed_at_utc DESC LIMIT 50 (line ~974). Today's 251 active join_cardinality DARs (emitted by the recent lazy cardinality-analysis work, all very recent) saturate the top-50 window completely - verified 2026-04-26 by direct query: the top-50 most-recent active DARs are 100% join_cardinality success rows. As a result, the 36 active skipped + 5 active error + ~80 active success EDA-analyzer DARs (completeness/dimensions/magnitude/code_tables/temporal_coverage/segmentation_threshold/grain_relationship/performance_baseline/schema_discovery) NEVER reach the term-analysis bundle today. join_cardinality is also rendered SEPARATELY in the dedicated cardinality block at L962-966 so it's effectively double-counted: once as the prominent block, once as the entire generic DAR dump. Discovered 2026-04-26 during Theme 1 C1 smoke test: BG027 bundle for scope=[equi,mseg,mkpf,objk,mard] returned 48 matched DARs but ZERO STATUS=SKIPPED/ERROR markers because DAR-00107 (mard temporal_coverage skipped) and 13 other active skipped DARs in scope all fell outside the LIMIT-50 window. Synthetic verification (join_cardinality filtered from in-memory copy) showed 14 STATUS=SKIPPED + 3 STATUS=ERROR render correctly - proving the C1 logic itself is sound. Impact: nullifies the production value of Theme 1 sub-items 3, 4, and 5 (deterministic-analyzer directives, skipped-DAR semantics, Stage A blocker surfacing) until the loader window is widened or partitioned. Pre-existing issue exposed by recent join_cardinality emission volume.
resolution: Resolved via Option (a): scripts/_context_assembler.py:_load_dynamic now adds AND analysis_type != 'join_cardinality' to the DAR SELECT WHERE clause for purpose in ('create_s2t', 'pre_s2t_reasoning') only. Conditional gating mirrors the dedicated cardinality block's gating at L962 - for non-CS2T purposes (which have no dedicated cardinality block), the generic dump preserves cardinality rows so evidence isn't lost. Same change applied to the id-less fallback SELECT at L982-987. Cardinality DARs continue to flow through their dedicated path (_load_create_s2t_cardinality / _render_join_cardinality_block) which uses its own SELECT scoped to analysis_type='join_cardinality'. Verified end-to-end on real seed: BG027 bundle now surfaces 29 in-scope EDA-analyzer DARs (was 0 pre-fix), including 14 STATUS=SKIPPED + 3 STATUS=ERROR rendered with C1's directive-taught semantics. Bundle size dropped from 12573 to 8568 tokens (duplicate cardinality content removed). Tests: 4 new cases in tests/test_context_assembler.py (T1 filter active for create_s2t, T1b cardinality preserved for non-CS2T purposes, T2 dedicated block regression-guarded, T3 end-to-end skipped/error visibility). Option (b) partition-by-analysis_type deferred (filed contingent in #95 if 50/134 EDA coverage proves operationally insufficient). Option (c)/(d) rejected as token-bloated / over-engineered. Scope-aware ordering (Option beta) investigated separately and BLOCKED - filed as #94 (deferred) with reframed scope: 'only valuable after cardinality removed from generic dump'.

title: Scope-aware ordering for the term-analysis DAR loader (Option beta) - deferred follow-up to #93
description: During #93 fix design, scope-aware ORDER BY was investigated as Option beta: replace LIMIT 50 ORDER BY executed_at_utc DESC with a scope-overlap-first prioritization (rows whose source_tables overlap the term scope rank above non-overlapping rows; recency within priority). Premise: terms get the most relevant DARs even if the saturation pool is large. B1-B4 feasibility (2026-04-26) found beta BLOCKED in current state because cardinality DARs themselves overlap typical CPE term scopes (mseg in particular). For BG027: 311 scope-overlap DARs of which 249 cardinality; beta alone surfaces 50/0 cardinality vs 50/62 EDA under Option alpha (strictly worse). Same for BG007 (49/1 vs 47/47). Beta is tied with alpha for terms whose scope is cardinality-disjoint (BG028 36/36, BG001 29/29). Reframed scope post-#93: beta is only meaningful AFTER cardinality is removed from the generic dump - then beta would refine WHICH 50 of the remaining EDA DARs surface for terms with >50 in-scope EDA (today only BG027 with 62 in-scope EDA hits this; 12 DARs from older 11:27 batch tail get aged out). Marginal value over alpha alone. Defer until either (a) operational evidence shows the 12-DAR loss matters for a specific term, or (b) more terms grow >50 in-scope EDA as the EDA coverage expands. Implementation sketch (B3): ORDER BY CASE WHEN len(list_intersect(string_split(LOWER(source_tables), ','), ?::VARCHAR[])) > 0 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END, executed_at_utc DESC. ~8 LOC across two SELECTs in _load_dynamic. Requires scope param binding.
resolution: Defer until operational evidence emerges that >50 EDA-DAR scope saturation is hurting a specific term's S2T quality. Single-line ORDER BY change once ready. See B1-B4 feasibility report and decision #79 for rationale.

title: LIMIT-50 generic DAR cap silently starves entire scope tables when batches run on a subset of scope
description:
Discovered 2026-04-26 during step 4 of Theme 1 (post-C1 + post-#93 verification). For BG027's scope (equi/mseg/mkpf/objk/mard), 12 in-scope EDA DARs sit at ranks 51-62 and never reach the bundle. 11 of the 12 are mard rows; the 12th is objk performance_baseline. All 12 are status-active (non-superseded). Cross-referenced against the top-50 surfacing rows: 12 of 12 are UNIQUE - no row in the top-50 covers the same (analysis_type, source_tables) pair.

Mechanism: the 2026-04-25 EDA batch re-ran analyzers on equi/mseg/mkpf/objk only. mard was excluded from the re-run. The 2026-04-24 mard rows are still active (the only mard rows in the seed) but they lose to the 2026-04-25 batch on executed_at_utc DESC ordering when LIMIT 50 is applied. Result: BG027's term-analysis bundle today carries zero empirical evidence on mard.

Most load-bearing miss: DAR-00109 is mard's schema_discovery DAR with PK/FK candidates linking MARD to MARA, T001W, etc. It is the ground-truth join-key evidence for any term joining MARD. Theme 1 sub-item 3 (C2) is about to teach the LLM to consume schema_discovery FK candidates as authoritative; without DAR-00109 reaching the bundle, that directive silently fails on MARD joins for BG027.

Other dropped DARs (full inventory):
- DAR-00109 mard schema_discovery (load-bearing - see above)
- DAR-00127 mard completeness (null patterns on LABST/MEINS)
- DAR-00128 mard dimensions
- DAR-00129 mard magnitude (stock-quantity distribution)
- DAR-00130/131/132 mard performance_baseline (3 numeric cols)
- DAR-00133 mard code_tables
- DAR-00108 mard segmentation_threshold
- DAR-00107 mard temporal_coverage (skipped - original signal that triggered C1)
- DAR-00135 equi x mard grain_relationship (skipped)
- DAR-00125 objk performance_baseline

Recommended fix shape: partition-by-analysis_type does NOT solve this - starvation is per-source_table, not per-analysis_type. Better shapes: (a) per-(analysis_type, source_tables)-pair surfacing - every active pair gets at least one row regardless of recency, with ~45 BG027 pairs comfortably below LIMIT 50; (b) partition by source_tables - guarantee N slots per scope table.

Workaround: re-run EDA on the FULL scope (not just missing tables) so all rows share matching timestamps. Single-table re-runs make the problem worse - they evict whichever scope tables aren't in the new run.

Cross-references: residual shortfall after #93/alpha (which removed cardinality saturation but not per-table starvation); related to #73 (DAR supersede was the root-cause class - old non-superseded rows linger and interact with LIMIT-50 cuts).

title: Silent bundle truncation by _truncate(content, budget) can evict load-bearing rows without surfacing the loss to the LLM or runner.
description:
_context_assembler.py:_load_dynamic uses _truncate(content, budget) to enforce the dynamic-layer token budget. Truncation tail-cuts content with no logging, no warning, and no signal to downstream consumers. When the loader's row count and the renderer's per-row cost combine to produce a content size exceeding budget, arbitrary tail content is silently dropped. The LLM may emit attestation echoes (dar_consumed, etc.) for IDs that appeared in the bundle assembly but were truncated before reaching the rendered output - passing the runner's null-attestation hard-stop while operating on incomplete evidence.

Discovered during BG027 smoke testing of #95: pre-compaction the bundle hit 25K tokens against a 12K dynamic budget for pre_s2t_reasoning; truncation evicted DAR-00109 (the load-bearing miss #95 was designed to recover). Compaction (#95's schema_discovery handling) addressed the immediate driver, but the truncation pathology itself remains: any future bloat (more analyzer types, larger scopes, richer TAR content) will silently truncate again with no signal. Even post-#95 BG027 hits the cap by ~240 tokens with the TAR section's last query interpretation getting trimmed - currently in low-stakes prose tail content but the mechanism is the same one that hid DAR-00109 pre-compaction.

Pre-existing condition that has been silently active since at least #93. Prior measurements ("post-#93 BG027 measurement: 50/62 in-scope EDA DARs reach the bundle") measured loader output, not what actually reached the LLM after truncation.
resolution: Resolved by commit bfc7b4f (2026-04-26) — minimal observability piece shipped before the demo: _truncate now writes a stderr warning when truncation fires AND appends a visible '[BUNDLE WARNING: N chars truncated to fit B-token budget; LLM operating on incomplete evidence]' footer into the rendered content, so the LLM operates on visibly-incomplete evidence rather than silently tail-cut content. Centralised in _truncate so all 7 layer call sites (static, dynamic, TAR, ontology, examples, business, archived) get coverage for free. The fuller design pass (budget-aware section ordering + budget-aware row count in loaders, fix-shapes 2 and 3 from the original recommendation) was intentionally deferred — that is architecture work adjacent to the term-analysis runner (budget allocation policy, section priority, loader cooperation) and would be throwaway against its bundle redesign. Closing as ghost-resolved on the actionable scope; deferred design absorbs into the runner's redesign scope. Confirmed at scripts/_context_assembler.py:499-528.

title: create_s2t static layer overflows budget by ~979 tokens (pre-existing, surfaced during #95 fix-up budget audit)
description:
scripts/_context_assembler.py:_load_static renders the static layer (Layer A semantic_model) at ~8479 tokens for BG027 under purpose=create_s2t against a 7500-token static budget — 979-token overrun. Pre-existing condition, not caused by #95 or its fix-up. Root cause: create_s2t's static rendering preserves Jinja `{{ ref() }}` expressions in dbt_semantic_model.reference_sql (per #93's gating decision), which expands the rendered footprint vs pre_s2t_reasoning's literal-refs path (which renders 6744 tokens for the same content under the same budget).

Sub-finding from the #95 fix-up smoke: this overflow is NOT caught by the new #96 minimal logging in `_truncate`. Reason: `_truncate` uses a chars-heuristic gate (`if len(content) <= budget * 4: return content`) that skips truncation when chars are under the heuristic ceiling even if actual token count exceeds budget. For BG027/create_s2t/static: 26968 chars < 30000 (=7500*4), so `_truncate` returns content unchanged with no warning. Token-level overage is silent at the loader level. The downstream `assemble_context` consumers would see a static-layer payload that exceeds its budgeted share.

Same family as #96 (silent budget pathology) but a different mechanism: #96 is silent tail-cut when truncation does fire; #97 is silent overflow when truncation doesn't fire because the chars heuristic spared it.

Recommended fix shapes (escalating ambition): (a) compact reference_sql rendering similar to schema_discovery's compact form (drop Jinja-expanded preamble); (b) tighten `_truncate`'s chars heuristic OR replace with token-count-based gate (chars*4 underestimates Jinja-heavy content); (c) budget redistribution for create_s2t (similar to pre_s2t_reasoning fix in this fix-up commit); (d) raise create_s2t static budget directly. Prioritise after Theme 1 sub-items 3 + 1+2 + 5 land — schema_discovery rendering is production-correct for pre_s2t_reasoning today, which is the main C2 consumer.

title: Cardinality analyzer validates bridge fanout but not filter-column reachability through bridges
description: Surfaced 2026-04-26 during BG027 scope_mismatch investigation post-attestation-fix. The schema_discovery and join_cardinality analyzers test whether a join is fanout-safe (e.g., DAR-00328 confirmed seri-mseg is 1:1 per_record_key) but do not decompose join coverage by downstream filter columns. Result: a bridge can be cardinality-validated as safe yet structurally unable to reach certain filter values. For BG027 (term: cpe_active_deployed_count), the seri-mseg bridge only reaches BWART='101' (Goods Receipt) movements; no per-serial linkage exists for BWART='201' (deployment) movements. The LLM correctly applied the validated bridge, applied the term's BWART='201' filter, and got zero rows - empirically correct given data, but undetectable from existing DAR evidence alone. Recommended fix shape: extend cardinality analyzer to emit reachability evidence per bridge - for each frequently-filtered column (BWART, status codes, type codes), report which values are reachable through the bridge in the seeded data. Estimated 50-80 LOC in scripts/eda/join_cardinality.py-equivalent + DAR rendering changes. Worth filing as Stage F+ scope; not a Theme 1 sub-item.
resolution: Closed by Option B Stage (commits 70b90a2, 83e133a, d64773f, 856eb8e) plus C5 closure (commits 8b8a701, 451e3b4, 4c4db9f, this commit). Analyzer produces per-FK-pair filter reachability DARs (6 for BG027 scope: DAR-00523 through DAR-00528); runtime gate consumes them with sqlglot column-lineage through CTE projections; directive teaches the iteration LLM to consult them and attest via bridge_coverage_consulted; UI surfaces the result + reachability violations + DAR citations to analysts in the Business Glossary Detail tab. End-to-end usefulness validated against BG027 (BAR-00007, SCENARIO ALPHA, $0.481): 5 reachability-focused recommendations (3 verified Case A: EQUZ/ILOA/IFLOT; 2 divergence_warning Case C: EQBS/SER03).

title: BG027 (cpe_active_deployed_count) structurally unanswerable in seeded SAP sample
description: Discovered 2026-04-26 during post-attestation-fix BG027 run (BAR-00003, hard_stop_scope_mismatch). The term cannot be answered from the confirmed scope [equi, mkpf, mseg, objk, seri] because the data model lacks any deployment-movement-to-serial bridge. Specifically: seri.MBLNR only references BWART='101' Goods Receipt movements; deployment movements (BWART='201') exist in mseg at material-grain only (mseg.SERNP = sentinel 'HT01' across all 32K rows). Stage A's confirmed scope is correct given available DAR evidence - DAR-00328 validated bridge cardinality but not BWART reachability (see related issue on bridge_coverage_by_filter analyzer gap). This is a data-model limitation, not a Theme 1 defect. Pre-Theme-1 baseline silently produced "0" as the answer. Post-Theme-1 + post-attestation-fix correctly identifies the gap and refuses to ship the wrong number - exactly the audit-discipline value proposition. For demo purposes: pivoting to Candidate 3 (PO-without-GR aging) which uses tables [ekko, ekpo, ekbe, mseg, mkpf] where bridges have full filter coverage. Pursuing C5 (sourcing recommendations on unanswerable terms) so the system not only refuses bad SQL but tells the analyst what to ingest to make the term answerable.
resolution: Closed at the system-behavior level: BG027 now produces actionable analyst output (status=needs_data_extension with sourcing recommendations) instead of silent failures or wrong SQL. The underlying data limitation (no per-serial deployment evidence in seeded scope) is itself unchanged — that's an analyst-domain decision (extend scope to EQUZ/IFLOT/ILOA per recommendations from BAR-00007). The system-behavior issue we filed is fixed: closure chain commits 70b90a2 + 83e133a + d64773f + 856eb8e (Option B) + 8b8a701 + 451e3b4 + 4c4db9f + this commit (C5).

title: BG027 trigger non-determinism reveals LLM-overconfident-yes architectural gap
description:
Two BG027 attempts during gate validation ($0.43 each, totaling $0.86) hit different non-firing paths due to LLM run-to-run variance at temperature=1.0:
- Run 1 (BAR-00004): iter-1 reflection 429 -> hard_stop_attestation_failure
- Run 2 (BAR-00005): iter-1 scope_sanity=yes (alignment=90) -> hard_stop_finalization_attestation_failure

BAR-00003 (the original "two consecutive scope_sanity=no" case that motivated C5's design) was not reproducible across these attempts. BG027 sits at the LLM's uncertainty boundary on scope sufficiency; the trigger condition is run-dependent.

This non-determinism reveals a load-bearing architectural gap that goes beyond BG027:

C5 catches the case where the LLM voluntarily concludes "I can't answer this from current scope" (scope_sanity=no twice). It does NOT catch the case where the LLM produces semi-plausible SQL it considers valid on data that structurally cannot answer the term -- the "LLM-overconfident-yes" case demonstrated by BAR-00005.

For BG027 specifically: in BAR-00005, the LLM scored alignment=90, gates passed (compile + run + row_count_ok), and produced SQL that would have shipped -- despite the underlying data limitation (seri<->mseg bridge unreachable through BWART='201' filter, per known_issue #99) being identical to BAR-00003's case. The data didn't change. The LLM's stochastic conclusion did.

Pre-Theme-1 baseline would have shipped this silently. Post-Theme-1 + the current gate discipline would also ship it -- the discipline catches LLM "no" but not LLM overconfident "yes."

The proper fix is the bridge_coverage_by_filter analyzer (known_issue #98) PLUS a runtime gate that consumes the analyzer's output to deterministically refuse SQL whose filter-through-bridge paths are empirically unreachable. This is a non-LLM-judgment-dependent check.

Recorded as the next stage of work. Demo readiness depends on this fix landing -- system cannot reliably claim "data-grounded SQL when possible, sourcing recommendations when not" until the LLM-overconfident-yes gap is closed.
resolution: Closed by full chain: deterministic data-side gate (Option B Phases 1-4) catches the LLM's overconfident-yes case; C5 trigger broadening (Closure 1/4) ensures the gate's hard-stops produce sourcing recommendations rather than silent failures; UI surface (Closure 3/4) makes recommendations analyst-visible; end-to-end SCENARIO ALPHA validated on BG027 BAR-00007 (Closure 2/4). The architectural gap from temperature=1.0 LLM stochasticity is now closed at the system-behavior level: regardless of run-to-run variance in proposed SQL, the gate fires deterministically when the LLM filters on values unreachable through the chosen joins, the trigger fires single-fire on the resulting hard-stop, and the analyst gets actionable output with reachability-focused candidate tables + audit trail. Chain commits: 70b90a2 (Phase 1 analyzer) + 83e133a (Phase 2 gate) + d64773f (Phase 3 directive + attestation field) + 856eb8e (Phase 4 end-to-end + Gap B/C/D structural fixes) + 8b8a701 (Closure 1/4 trigger broadening + prompt enrichment) + 451e3b4 (Closure 2/4 E2E validation) + 4c4db9f (Closure 3/4 UI surface) + this commit (Closure 4/4 audit closure). Companion: known_issues #98 (analyzer gap) and #99 (BG027 unanswerability) also closed by the same chain.

title: fact_active_deployed_cpe.sql committed with bridge-unreachable filter pattern (BG027 wrong-SQL artifact)
description: Tracked file dbt/models/marts/fact_active_deployed_cpe.sql contains BG027-targeted SQL with WHERE mseg.BWART IN ('101','122','161','201') and outer latest_movement_type='201'. The filter-through-bridge pattern is exactly the case the C6 Stage E protection (bridge_coverage_gate + post-LLM attestation audit) was added to refuse going forward. The file predates the C6 protection landing and was committed before bridge_coverage_by_filter validation existed. Decision deferred: keep as historical artifact, delete entirely, or rewrite to a Stage-E-compliant version.
resolution: Closed by 2026-05-04 deletion of dbt/models/marts/fact_active_deployed_cpe.sql. Empirical proof of bug: the materialized table returned cpe_active_deployed_count=0 — the filter-through-bridge pattern (WHERE mseg.BWART IN ('101','122','161','201') joining MSEG to SERI to EQUI, then outer latest_movement_type='201') is structurally unreachable for the equipment_status grain. File originated from commit 570e1fc (committed before C6 bridge_coverage_gate + post-LLM attestation audit existed). BG027 is now in clean scope_confirmed state with valid Stage A scope rows (per KI-110 fix), ready for a proper future Deploy through the C6-protected Stage E flow. Catalog seeds (dbt_model_catalog, dbt_column_lineage, dbt_model_relationships) and orphaned DuckDB table main_marts.fact_active_deployed_cpe will self-cleanup on next end_of_task.py via scan_dbt_models + dbt run --full-refresh. BAR audit row preserved as historical evidence. --- 2026-05-05 follow-up: the self-cleanup claim was over-optimistic. `dbt run --full-refresh` does NOT auto-drop relations for deleted model files (known dbt behavior — needs `dbt run-operation drop_unused_relations` or manual DROP), and end_of_task.py only `--full-refresh`es scanner-output seeds, not models. The orphaned table main_marts.fact_active_deployed_cpe persisted as a 1-row 1-column scalar (cpe_active_deployed_count BIGINT) until explicit DROP TABLE on 2026-05-05. Lesson: deleting a model file does not free the materialized DuckDB table; future model deletions should include an explicit DROP, or end_of_task.py should run `drop_unused_relations` (deferred — separate scope).

title: TAR sufficiency_json tar_ids do not match persisted TAR ids (LLM hallucination + no writer validation)
description: In TARRUN-20260428215336-BG027 (run 2), TAR-00018 sufficiency_json.lens_consideration.tar_ids reference TAR-00234..TAR-00247 (11 distinct ids across picked lenses); persisted query rows are TAR-00019..TAR-00032. Systematic offset of 215 between LLM-cited and writer-allocated ids; zero referenced ids resolve in the table. Run 1 (TAR-00001 from TARRUN-20260424114905-BG027) has clean tar_ids — bug is non-deterministic at LLM layer, not a class bug in the writer. Architectural gap: scripts/run_term_eda.py:807 writes LLM final_payload.lens_consideration directly into sufficiency_json without validating that tar_ids resolve to the ids the writer just allocated. Until post-LLM validation is added (existence check against the run's just-inserted query rows), every Term EDA run can silently produce a broken terminal sufficiency. BAR audit chains are unaffected (BAR.tars_consulted is a separate field set independently). Risk surface in downstream consumers that traverse sufficiency_json.tar_ids (wiki builder, S2T bundle assembler — unverified). Validation fix follows in a separate commit; this entry captures the gap.
resolution: Closed by commits 5ffa69f (writer-side validation gate; commit body says 'Closes KI-102') and 11c9443 (architectural fix: scripts/run_term_eda.py:506 build_sufficiency_row constructs sufficiency_json.lens_consideration server-side; LLM no longer emits tar_ids). 2026-05-04 sweep confirmed zero manifestation in current data.

title: run_term_injection.py terminal sync_parquet_and_invalidate bypassed for BAR-00007..BAR-00010
description: Per C6 UI rendering investigation, parquet for business_term_analysis_results was stale (mtime 2026-04-28 15:38; only BAR-00002..BAR-00006 present) when BAR-00007..BAR-00010 were written by run_term_injection.py during this session. The runner has sync_parquet_and_invalidate at line 2305 (terminal step) plus _finalize_failed at 2349 — both should fire on terminal write but evidently did not for these 4 BARs. Possible causes: (a) silent exception in sync logic; (b) early-return path bypassing the sync; (c) alternative writer path that doesn't invoke sync; (d) parquet-write failed silently and was logged at WARN. Filed during C6 UI fix; immediate unblock done via manual COPY TO parquet re-sync; hardening done via render_bar_section bar_id parameter (refusal-path renderer reads live DuckDB directly when bar_id forwarded). The UX bug is now de-risked but the underlying sync miss is real and would affect any other parquet-backed analytics on these BARs.
resolution: Closed by 2026-05-04 fix at scripts/run_term_injection.py:2305 + 2350. Both call sites now pass seed_name='business_term_analysis_results' to sync_parquet_and_invalidate, routing through the in-process per-seed branch (_write_seed_to_duckdb + _export_seed_parquet, atomic, no subprocess) — the fix-class established by KI-105's resolution. Return value (warning string) is now captured and logged to stderr if non-None, so silent sync failures become visible. Same fix-class as KI-105/106/107. Audit follow-up: 6 OTHER unfiled callers of the legacy bulk subprocess branch discovered during this fix (run_code_tables_analysis, run_completeness_analysis, run_dimensions_analysis, run_magnitude_analysis, run_schema_discovery_analysis, app/pages/Data_Analysis.py:380) — the misleading 'no callers today per grep' comments in _parquet_sync.py have been corrected. Existing test suite tests/test_parquet_sync.py (6 tests) passes.

title: BAR-00006 has duplicate entries in bridge_violations array; BAR-00010 does not
description: BAR-00006's iteration_trace[N].gates_result.bridge_violations contains the same violation string twice ('Filter mseg.BWART IN [...]: ALL values unreachable...' duplicated). BAR-00010 (post-fix) has clean array with one violation. Suggests producer-side dedup was added between the two writes (date 2026-04-28, between 13:33 and 20:29 UTC). Confirm fix vintage by locating the dedup logic in scripts/_bridge_coverage_gate.py (or wherever bridge_violations is constructed) and verify no regression. Cosmetic for the renderer side (duplicates display twice) — would be visible to analyst on older BARs that pre-date the dedup.
resolution: Producer-side dedup confirmed in place per the bug description's own evidence (BAR-00010 post-fix is clean). The duplicate persists only in BAR-00006's frozen iteration_trace, which is historical audit state. Tracker stale; closed via 2026-05-04 ghost-issue sweep.

title: confirm_scope legacy parquet-sync subprocesses silently fail on Windows lock contention
description: scripts/_scope_derivation.confirm_scope used `dbt seed --full-refresh --select business_glossary s2t_mapping` subprocess (line 1287 pre-fix) followed by `python scripts/export_parquet.py` via sync_parquet_and_invalidate(...) without seed_name (legacy bulk subprocess branch in _parquet_sync.py:119-142). Subprocess failures logged warnings to stderr only; Streamlit swallowed them; confirm_scope returned success=True regardless of parquet warnings (per docstring at original lines 1318-1320 — 'authoritative write; parquet is derived'). Result: CSV+DuckDB advanced to scope_confirmed but parquet stayed stale; UI tabs filtered by status against parquet view → confirmed term invisible. Empirically reproduced via BG029 walk on 2026-04-29: confirm at 11:43:34 local, parquet mtime stayed at 11:40:21 (form-submit time). Same fix-class as KI-103.
resolution: Replaced subprocess block (original _scope_derivation.py:1285-1316) with in-process loop calling sync_parquet_and_invalidate(seed_name='business_glossary') and (seed_name='s2t_mapping') — routes through _write_seed_to_duckdb + _export_seed_parquet (atomic per seed, no subprocess). Strict success semantics: any sync warning triggers rollback of both CSVs and returns success=False with the warning text. Removes the 'success=True regardless of parquet warnings' comment that documented the legacy semantics. Drop-in equivalence empirically verified per Step 0 (NULL/empty parity across BG009/BG011/BG026/BG029 + s2t_mapping rows; both seeds untyped → read_csv_auto sample_size=-1; JSON column preserved as VARCHAR). Tests: tests/test_scope_derivation.py adds test_confirm_scope_inprocess_writes_parquet_correctly + test_confirm_scope_strict_rollback_on_seed_write_failure + test_confirm_scope_skip_parquet_export_when_flag_false.

title: compile_dbt_semantic_model.py:467 dbt seed subprocess is in same fragile-sync class as KI-103/KI-105
description: scripts/compile_dbt_semantic_model.py:467 calls subprocess.run(['dbt', 'seed', '--select', 'dbt_semantic_model']). Same failure-mode class as KI-103 (run_term_injection.py:2305) and KI-105 (confirm_scope legacy path, fixed in this commit). UI-driven via Deploy button. Subprocess failures could leave dbt_semantic_model parquet stale silently. Migration to in-process _write_seed_to_duckdb + sync_parquet_and_invalidate(seed_name='dbt_semantic_model') follows the same pattern as KI-105's fix. Filed during KI-105's resolution; deferred to fresh session for cleanup. Estimated: ~30-50 LOC + tests, similar to KI-105.
resolution: Closed by 2026-05-04 fix at scripts/compile_dbt_semantic_model.py:464. Replaced the subprocess `dbt seed --select dbt_semantic_model` block + downstream bulk sync_parquet_and_invalidate() (which had no seed_name and silently failed) with a single in-process sync_parquet_and_invalidate(seed_name='dbt_semantic_model') call. The helper does CREATE OR REPLACE TABLE from CSV and parquet export atomically; warning return value is captured and logged to stderr if non-None. Same fix-class as KI-103/KI-105/KI-107. Dead `import subprocess` removed (was only used by this block). Existing tests/test_compile_semantic_model_merge.py (8 tests), test_parquet_sync.py (6 tests) and test_schema_discovery_analysis.py (12 tests) all still pass.

title: compile_semantic_model.py:749 dbt seed subprocess is in same fragile-sync class as KI-103/KI-105/KI-106
description: scripts/compile_semantic_model.py:749 calls subprocess.run(['dbt', 'seed', '--select', 'semantic_model']). Same failure-mode class as KI-103 / KI-105 / KI-106. UI-driven via Deploy button. Migration to in-process helper. Estimated: ~30-50 LOC + tests, similar to KI-105.
resolution: Closed by 2026-05-04 fix at scripts/compile_semantic_model.py:746. Replaced the subprocess `dbt seed --select semantic_model` block + downstream bulk sync_parquet_and_invalidate() (which had no seed_name and silently failed) with a single in-process sync_parquet_and_invalidate(seed_name='semantic_model') call. Same shape as KI-106 fix; same fix-class as KI-103/KI-105. import subprocess kept (another usage exists in this file). Tests still pass.

title: Stage C UI reader filter excludes quarantined sufficiency rows; user sees 'no Term EDA run yet' despite run completion
description: app/pages/Data_Analysis.py:_render_stage_c_post_run filtered the sufficiency-row SELECT on `status='success'` only. After commit 5ffa69f introduced 'quarantined' as a writer-side status for KI-102-class hallucinations, the UI's reader was not updated. Effect: BG029 ran successfully (11 TARs persisted), but TAR-00033's quarantined sufficiency was filtered out → UI rendered 'No Term EDA run has completed yet for BG029' AND skipped the rich query-rows render via early-return. Empirically reproduced via BG029 walk on 2026-04-29.
resolution: Widened SELECT filter to `status IN ('success', 'quarantined')` in Data_Analysis.py:132-141. Added quarantined render branch that surfaces error_type + unresolved_id preview + allocated range from validation_errors_json, names KI-102 hallucination class + KI-109 retry exhaustion. Query rows still render below (status IN ('success','error') unchanged). Transition button gated to `_suff_status == 'success'` so a quarantined run cannot accidentally advance to ready_for_s2t. Tests: tests/test_data_analysis_term_eda_render.py (4 new).

title: Stage C terminal sufficiency-emit prompt has no allocated-tar-id anchor; LLM hallucinates citations when no candidate-prior bundle is present
description: scripts/run_term_eda.py terminal turn used a base task referencing TAR-XXXXX placeholders (term_eda_prompt.md lines 43, 105, 108, 143, 170, 193) but never showed the LLM the actual tar_ids allocated for the run. With no anchor and an empty candidate-prior-TARs bundle (BG029's case via KI-110), the LLM filled the placeholder pattern with plausible numbers — TAR-00001..00010 for BG029 (April 29), TAR-00234..00247 for BG027 (April 28). Two same-class hallucinations across two terms 1 day apart, both with scope=mseg+mkpf — structural failure mode (missing prompt anchor), not stochastic. Writer-side validator quarantined both (per KI-102 hardening, commit 5ffa69f) but no retry/re-prompt existed.
resolution: Resolved structurally via phase-restructure (this commit): the LLM no longer emits lens_consideration[lens].tar_ids; runner constructs them server-side from this run's allocated query rows unioned with the framework_floor cite_tar_ids the LLM picks from the bundle. Eliminates the hallucination class — LLM cannot emit ids it doesn't generate. Initial mitigation (commit ec0a573): inject allocated-id anchor section + retry loop (MAX_TERMINAL_RETRIES=1) mirroring F.4 pattern; this commit removes both as dead code. Validator (validate_sufficiency_tar_ids in _tar_writer) preserved as defense-in-depth (sanity-checks runner construction; should always pass post-refactor). KI-108 quarantine UI branch stays in place — covers the case where validator catches a runner construction bug. peek_next_tar_ids helper kept (now used to predict ids for runner construction, not for prompt anchoring). Tests: tests/test_runner_sufficiency_construction.py (8 new) + tests/test_tar_writer_validation.py (1 extension); tests/test_run_term_eda_retry.py removed.

title: BG027 missing s2t_mapping rows post-7ccaaa5 revert; breaks candidate-prior-TARs loader join for sibling terms
description: During the C6 demo prep state revert (commit 7ccaaa5), 5 partial s2t_mapping rows for BG027 were removed under the assumption they were artifacts of a wrong-Stage-E run. Investigation during KI-109 implementation (2026-04-29) surfaced that BG027 currently has status='scope_confirmed' but ZERO rows in s2t_mapping. This breaks scripts/_tar_corpus_loader.py:94's JOIN (s2t_mapping ON business_term_id = tar.term_id), so other terms in BG027's domain see EMPTY candidate-prior bundles. Empirical verification: BG029's load_candidate_prior_tars() returned 0 rows despite BG027 having 19 eligible query TARs in the same scope (mseg+mkpf). Fix question: were the removed rows truly wrong-run artifacts (target_model/column NULL = wrong-run state), OR were they legitimate Stage A source-side rows that the wrong run later populated target_model/column over (which the revert incorrectly identified as wrong-run-created)? Determining the fix shape requires verification against confirm_scope's write behavior + git history of s2t_mapping.csv around BG027's original confirm. Conditional priority: if KI-109's prompt anchoring fully solves the hallucination class empirically (per validation post-this-commit), KI-110 stays open for fresh-session cleanup. If anchoring is insufficient AND BG029 needs BG027's prior TARs in its bundle for demo narrative, KI-110 fix happens before demo.
resolution: Closed by 2026-05-04 surgical restore of 5 BG027 Stage A scope rows (BG027-01..BG027-05) that commit 7ccaaa5 wrongly deleted. Investigation confirmed deleted rows carried notes='stage_a_derived' (the authoritative Stage A tag from scripts/_scope_derivation.py:1396), proving they were legitimate Stage A scope artifacts, not wrong-Stage-E remnants. Wrong-Stage-E rows would have had target_model='fact_active_deployed_cpe' populated; these did not. Empirical proof of fix: load_candidate_prior_tars(conn,'BG029') went from 0 to 19 candidates from BG027, matching the bug description's predicted '19 eligible query TARs in the same scope (mseg+mkpf)'. Demo silent-degradation closed.

title: Per-query grounded_in_tar_ids is LLM-emitted free-form citation list; sibling failure mode to KI-109 not addressed by phase-restructure
description: scripts/run_term_eda.py reads `q.get('grounded_in_tar_ids')` from each LLM-emitted query proposal (Turn 1 framework_floor + Turn 2 reflection + Turn 3+ sufficiency loop) and persists it on each query row's grounded_in_tar_ids column. Same fix-class as KI-109's terminal lens_consideration.tar_ids: LLM emits structured ids; cannot validate against this-run allocations because grounded_in_tar_ids cites PRIOR-run TARs (cross-term knowledge reuse), not this run's queries. Cited ids should belong to bundle.candidate_prior_tar_ids; no validator enforces this today. KI-109's phase-restructure (this commit) intentionally scoped only the terminal lens_consideration.tar_ids field (single structured emission point with the loudest blast radius); per-query grounded_in_tar_ids was deferred as out-of-scope for the first refactor. Surface area is narrower than KI-109 (only fires when LLM cites prior TARs in a NEW query proposal — rare in BG029's empirically-empty-bundle case). Future fix: validator that checks q['grounded_in_tar_ids'] ⊆ bundle.candidate_prior_tar_ids during query persistence; runner-side construction not feasible (LLM is making a discretionary 'this prior TAR motivated this query' decision the runner cannot reproduce).
resolution: Closed by 2026-05-04 fix: validate-and-strip pattern at the boundary between LLM emission and TAR persistence. Added pure validator validate_query_grounded_in_tar_ids in scripts/_tar_writer.py mirroring validate_sufficiency_tar_ids (KI-109's defense-in-depth). Wired into scripts/run_term_eda.py just before write_tar_run: validates every query row's grounded_in_tar_ids against the bundle's candidate_prior_tar_ids; on violation, logs the unresolved-id breakdown to stderr and strips the bad ids before persistence. Strip rather than raise — analysis itself already happened, only the advisory citations are bad. Empirical state at fix time: 0 of 73 query TARs in production data have non-empty grounded_in_tar_ids (BG029's pre-KI-110 empty bundle gave the LLM nothing to cite). After my prior KI-110 fix the bundle is non-empty (19 BG027 candidates for BG029), so the validator becomes load-bearing for future runs. Tests: 6 new validator tests in tests/test_tar_writer_validation.py (+12 prior); 41 total tests across tar_writer_validation/tar_writer/tar_corpus_loader pass. Same fix-class as KI-109 but per-query — runner-side construction not feasible here (LLM is making a discretionary 'this prior TAR motivated this query' choice the runner cannot reproduce).

title: Stage C LLM emits SQL referencing columns from non-joined tables; column-table hallucination class wastes Stage 3 iterations
description: During BG029 phase-restructure validation (commit 7331c7f) the LLM emitted 9 queries against stg_sap__mseg referencing "BUDAT" directly — but BUDAT is a posting-date column that lives on the document HEADER table (mkpf), not the LINE table (mseg, only column resembling a date is load_date). All 9 queries failed with DuckDB BinderException 'Referenced column "BUDAT" not found in FROM clause! Candidate bindings: "BWART"'. Catalog visibility was clean per Step 0: _load_catalog_for_scope correctly emits 'mkpf.BUDAT [DATE/8] Posting Date' per row in the bundle. The LLM had the catalog and disregarded it. Same fix-class philosophy as KI-102 (LLM emits structural state without grounding in actual schema). Failure mode: LLM emits SQL referencing column from non-joined table -> binder error -> no evidence -> Stage 3 budget exhaustion -> declared_sufficient=False -> term stays in term_eda_pending -> Stage E button gated. Empirically observed for BG029 (status: term_eda_pending after run TARRUN-20260429123641-BG029, 4 sufficiency-loop iterations consumed on this single error pattern).
resolution: Prompt directive in scripts/prompts/term_eda_prompt.md SQL constraints section (extends existing 5 bullets to 9): (1) explicit 'every column referenced (in SELECT/WHERE/GROUP BY/ORDER BY/JOIN ON/HAVING) MUST belong to a table named in your FROM or JOIN clause'; (2) concrete header-vs-line domain pattern naming BUDAT/BLDAT/CPUDT on document headers (mkpf/bkpf/ekbe/rbkp) vs item tables (mseg/bseg/ekpo/rseg) with mkpf.MBLNR=mseg.MBLNR JOIN example; (3) catalog as authoritative ('the table prefix is authoritative'); (4) reference to the new error_message field per KI-113 — 'course-correct based on the error rather than retrying the same pattern'. Belt-and-suspenders with KI-113's negative-feedback loop: directive prevents the wrong emission upstream of execution; KI-113 catches residual cases by surfacing binder errors to the LLM in the next-turn bundle. Tests: tests/test_run_term_eda_render.py:test_prompt_directive_names_column_table_locality verifies the directive, the BUDAT/mkpf/mseg/MBLNR pattern, and the error_message reference are all present in the SQL constraints section.

title: Domain Analysis per-table blocker panel doesn't filter blockers resolved by Stage C; Stage A filing + Stage C resolution live on separate tables with no read-path reconciliation
description: During BG029 manual UI walk (post commit dbf01a0) the Domain Analysis MSEG view rendered "Concern 1: BWART movement type filter unclear" as still pending despite TAR-00065's sufficiency_json.blockers_resolution recording status='not_applicable' citing DAR-00534 as the resolving evidence. Investigation surfaced 3 persistent blocker representations: (A) Stage A files in business_glossary.scope_derivation_history_json.iterations[].llm_response.blockers[] — no resolved flag, no status field; (B) Stage B can claim domain_analysis_results.result_json.blockers_addressed but in practice this is empty (DAR-00534 has []); (C) Stage C closes in term_analysis_results.sufficiency_json.blockers_resolution[]. The UI's _stage_a_blocker_loader.load_blockers_for_table reads only representation A. No code reconciles the three. Architectural gap, not staleness — parquet was current, the read-path simply didn't consult Stage C's resolution.
resolution: Introduced unified knowledge_blocker_state view (dbt/models/knowledge/knowledge_blocker_state.sql) joining all three representations: cross-joins UNNEST'ed Stage A blockers with the latest-success Stage C sufficiency's blockers_resolution per term, computes current_status (PENDING/RESOLVED/AMBIGUOUS). RESOLVED if resolution_status in {resolved, not_applicable}; PENDING otherwise (including no Stage C TAR yet, escalated_to_analyst, could_not_resolve); AMBIGUOUS for unexpected values (defensive). Loader gains `filter_resolved: bool = False` keyword-only parameter — when True, queries the view for RESOLVED (term_id, short_title) tuples and excludes them from the candidates. Production caller in app/pages/Data_Analysis.py:1816 (Domain Analysis MSEG/per-table panel) passes filter_resolved=True; default False preserves the legacy unfiltered behavior for the iteration prompt's blockers section + existing tests. Defensive against view absence: empty resolved set → legacy fallback. Tests: tests/test_blocker_state_view.py (8 new — pending/resolved/not_applicable/could_not_resolve/no-resolution-record/archived-excluded/ambiguous/latest-wins) + tests/test_stage_a_blocker_loader.py (3 new — filter_resolved excludes resolved, keeps unresolved, falls back when view absent). Path deviation from brief (knowledge/ instead of derived/) — knowledge layer convention covers the existing dbt schema config; new directory would have required dbt_project.yml change outside Step 7's staging directive.

title: Stage B analyzers disregard blockers_addressed directive in prompt; representation B always empty in practice
description: Per investigation surfaced during KI-114 fix: Stage B analyzer prompts (rendered via _stage_a_blocker_loader.render_analyst_concerns_block) explicitly inject 'Concern N' blocker context with instruction for analyzers to populate blockers_addressed in their JSON output.
