Empirically, analyzers disregard this — DAR-00534 (the analyzer Stage C cited as resolving BG029's BWART blocker) has blockers_addressed=[]. This makes Stage B's representation non-load-bearing for blocker resolution; Stage C ends up doing all resolution work via blockers_resolution. KI-114's unified view treats the empty array as expected (resolution comes from Stage C), but the directive disregard is a separate issue. Same fix-class as KI-102/KI-112: LLM disregards directive without consequence. Investigation needed: (a) Should we strengthen the prompt directive at Stage B to make analyzers actually populate? (b) Or accept representation B as defunct and remove the prompt section + simplify the lifecycle to A→C only? Filed during KI-114 implementation; deferred for fresh-session design discussion.
resolution: Closed by 2026-05-04 fix at scripts/_stage_a_blocker_loader.py:266 — render_analyst_concerns_block. Removed the dead `blockers_addressed` directive from the rendered Stage B analyst-concerns block (the opening 'Address each one via the blockers_addressed field' instruction and the closing paragraph). Concern context (term attribution + 6 augmentation fields per blocker) retained for analyst awareness. The field stays in DAR result_json schema for backward compatibility but is intentionally always-empty going forward. The downstream knowledge_blocker_state view (KI-114) already consumes only Stage A blockers + Stage C blockers_resolution, never representation B — its docstring updated to reflect that representation B is now intentionally absent (was: 'empirically empty'). Test test_12_render_non_empty_shape rewritten to assert (negatively) that the dead directive does not reappear. All 28 tests in tests/test_stage_a_blocker_loader.py pass. Audit follow-up tracked as KI-119: regression scenario_23_stage_b_blocker_injection in scripts/regression_piece8.py (~384 LOC) tests this dead path and would fail if invoked; out of scope for this fix's bounded change.

title: grain_relationship skip rationale opaque to analysts; many SAP pairs legitimately skip but UI doesn't explain why
description: Per investigation: scripts/run_grain_relationship_analysis.py:322-331 skips pairs that don't share numeric columns. SAP MM topology has many such pairs (header-line, dimension-fact) where grain_relationship doesn't apply by analyzer design. Currently UI shows 'Skipped (see collapsible)' with skip_reason in the expander, but analysts may not know this is expected behavior for header-line topology vs an analysis gap. Cosmetic enhancement: tooltip or inline help explaining grain_relationship is for measure-comparison; header-line pairs handled by join_cardinality + bridge_coverage_by_filter instead. Filed during KI-114 implementation; cosmetic; deferred.
resolution: Closed by 2026-05-04 cosmetic enhancement at app/_dar_render.py _render_skipped_dar. When a skipped DAR has analysis_type='grain_relationship', a contextual st.caption now renders below the skip-reason banner explaining: 'grain_relationship compares measures between tables that share numeric columns. Header-line and dimension-fact pairs (typical for SAP MM topology) are covered by join_cardinality and bridge_coverage_by_filter instead — this skip is expected behavior, not a coverage gap.' Hint is analyzer-scoped (does not appear for other analyzers' skips). 2 new unit tests verify both render and non-render paths in tests/test_dar_render.py (16 total tests pass). VISUAL VERIFICATION NOTE: I cannot drive a browser; per CLAUDE.md 'if you can't test the UI, say so explicitly' — the analyst-facing rendering should be eyeballed in Streamlit on a term whose Domain Analysis tab shows a skipped grain_relationship DAR (e.g. any header-line MSEG/MKPF or EQUI/SERI pair).

title: Stage C runner captures DuckDB error string but build_query_row drops it; LLM never sees why queries failed
description: scripts/run_term_eda.py:_execute_query catches binder errors at conn.execute and returns dict with status='error' + error key carrying the full DuckDB error string (e.g., 'BinderException: Referenced column "BUDAT" not found'). But scripts/_tar_writer.py:build_query_row had no error_message parameter — the captured error was dropped at row construction. Persisted query row had status='error' + empty query_result_json + no error reason. Bundle assembly via _render_query_results showed 'status=error, result_rows=0' but no error message. LLM's next-turn context had no signal explaining the failure → LLM blindly retried variants of the same wrong SQL pattern. Empirically: BG029 phase-restructure validation run consumed 4 sufficiency-loop iterations + 10 stage-3 queries on the same column-table mismatch (KI-112) without ever course-correcting because the negative feedback was invisible. Stage B has had error_message in domain_analysis_results for some time (schema.yml:361); Stage C was the only stage missing this convention.
resolution: Mirrored Stage B's existing convention. Added error_message column to term_analysis_results (schema.yml + TAR_FIELDS, 20→21 columns); existing 64 historical rows backfilled with empty error_message (DuckDB reads as NULL). build_query_row + build_sufficiency_row accept error_message parameter (default empty); sufficiency rows always empty per OQ-1 (schema symmetry). Three call sites in run_term_eda.py thread exec_result.get('error', '') through to build_query_row. _render_query_results emits 'error_message: <text>' line (truncated to 600 chars to bound bundle bloat) when status='error' AND error_message non-empty; omits the line for successful rows or rows without an error string (defensive against historical pre-fix rows). Combined with KI-112's prompt directive, gives the LLM both prevention (don't emit) and correction (here's what went wrong if you did). Tests: tests/test_tar_writer_validation.py adds 4 (build_query_row error_message present/default; sufficiency error_message always empty; CSV round-trip persistence); tests/test_run_term_eda_render.py adds 6 (render error line presence/omission/truncation/mixed; runner threading; prompt directive verification per KI-112).

title: Staging-layer compiled-SQL display previously showed 'not yet compiled' for deployed models; fixed via DuckDB-primary check
description: Per investigation surfaced during BG029 demo walk: Business_Glossary.py:2422-2429 _read_compiled() inspected dbt/target/compiled/cpe_procurement_analytics/models/<layer>/<model>.sql filesystem cache to determine compilation state. Profile data (rows/cols) was read from live DuckDB. Two state sources didn't reconcile: when the compiled cache was stale (no dbt compile run since last clean) but the table was deployed and queryable, UI rendered 'not yet compiled' adjacent to profile data showing 31,965 rows. Empirically reproduced for stg_sap__mseg + stg_sap__mkpf during BG029 Stage E S2T render — both tables had 31,965 rows in main_staging but no compiled .sql in target/compiled/.../staging/.
resolution: Reordered check logic. New helper module app/_compiled_state.py provides: load_deployed_keys(conn) snapshotting (table_schema, table_name) pairs from information_schema.tables; is_deployed(model_name, layer, deployed_keys) for O(1) lookup; read_compiled_sql(model_name, compiled_base) (relocated from inline _read_compiled). Render branch in app/pages/Business_Glossary.py now distinguishes three states: (1) deployed in DuckDB (✅) — primary expected state; show compiled SQL inline if cache available, caption noting `dbt compile --select <model>` would refresh the cache otherwise (deployed table is unaffected); (2) compiled but not deployed (⚙️) — pre-deployment workflow; show SQL; (3) neither (❌) — truly unaccounted-for. Eliminates the misleading 'not yet compiled' text for deployed models. Same UX-staleness fix-class as KI-108 (UI reader/writer enum drift); different state domain (filesystem cache vs DuckDB metadata). Tests: tests/test_business_glossary_compiled_render.py (6 new) cover is_deployed pure-lookup, load_deployed_keys real-DuckDB + error fallback, read_compiled_sql found/absent/missing-base.

title: run_schema_discovery_analysis silently skipped parquet sync via wrong kwarg name (seeds_changed)
description: scripts/run_schema_discovery_analysis.py:814 (pre-fix line) called sync_parquet_and_invalidate(..., seeds_changed=['domain_analysis_results']). seeds_changed is not a valid kwarg; Python raised TypeError. A try/except wrapped the call and printed `[warn] parquet sync skipped: <TypeError>` to stdout, making the failure look benign. Result: every successful schema_discovery run left domain_analysis_results parquet stale relative to DuckDB. Same fix-class as KI-103/KI-105/KI-106/KI-107. Discovered during KI-103 audit follow-up; resolved in same commit as KI-106/KI-107 fix.
resolution: Closed by 2026-05-04 fix at scripts/run_schema_discovery_analysis.py:811. Discovered during KI-103 audit follow-up: the call passed seeds_changed=[...] which is not a valid kwarg for sync_parquet_and_invalidate. The TypeError was caught by a benign-looking try/except that printed `[warn] parquet sync skipped: <TypeError>` to stdout, leaving parquet stale every run. Fix renames the kwarg to seed_name='domain_analysis_results' and unwraps from list. Captures the function's own warning return value. Same fix-class as KI-103/KI-105/KI-106/KI-107.

title: scenario_23_stage_b_blocker_injection regression test exercises dead code path post KI-115
description: scripts/regression_piece8.py:1752 — scenario_23_stage_b_blocker_injection is an LLM-dependent regression test (~384 lines) that asserts DAR.result_json.blockers_addressed is non-empty when a blocker is fabricated. KI-115 closure removed the prompt directive that asked analyzers to populate this field; the test now exercises a dead code path and would fail if invoked (it's marked 'R/LLM-dependent' so does not run in any automated suite — manual/on-demand only). Cleanup: remove scenario_23 + its registration entry at line 2837. Bounded (~385 LOC delete + 1-line registration); cleanly owned by KI-115.

title: Wire orphan-relation cleanup into end_of_task.py so deleted models auto-drop
description: When a dbt model file is deleted (e.g., KI-101 deleted fact_active_deployed_cpe.sql on 2026-05-04), the materialized table in DuckDB persists as an orphan because dbt does NOT auto-drop relations for deleted model files. The orphan stays until someone explicitly DROPs it. Today's incident (2026-05-05): user spotted main_marts.fact_active_deployed_cpe as a 1-column orphan during the schema-audit conversation; explicit DROP TABLE was needed to clean it up. KI-101's original resolution claimed self-cleanup via `dbt run --full-refresh`, but that's not how dbt works — amended in commit fcc575b. Fix shape: add a step to end_of_task.py (or a separate scripts/cleanup_orphans.py callable from there) that compares the materialized tables in DuckDB against the dbt graph (manifest.json or `dbt list`) and DROPs the diff. Implementation is not trivial: dbt-utils does NOT ship a drop_unused_relations macro (verified 2026-05-05); options are (a) author a custom macro that reads graph.nodes + information_schema, (b) write Python that loads dbt's manifest.json and DROPs the diff. False-positive risk: must exclude raw_sap.* sources, main_seeds.*, manually-managed views, and anything else not built by `dbt run` — naive 'all tables not in graph' would wipe sources. Could gate on layer prefix (main_marts/main_obt/main_knowledge) or on dbt-managed-schema list. Cosmetic-priority: orphans don't break correctness; they just clutter the schema audit and confuse analysts who notice them.

title: fact_po_invoice_match_variance refactored — vault layers built (RSEG + ZMM)
description: fact_po_invoice_match_variance refs stg_sap__ekko, stg_sap__ekpo, stg_sap__ekbe, stg_sap__rseg AND directly references raw_sap.zmm_approval_log (Layer 0 -> Layer 3 skip — worse than the staging-bypass since it skips both staging AND vault). Refactor blocked because: (1) no vault sat exists for RSEG (invoice line items) — only sat_invoice_header is in vault; (2) zmm_approval_log Z-table has no staging or vault model at all; (3) sat_gr_item could substitute for stg_sap__ekbe BWART='101' rows but it's a substitute, not a 1:1 vault analog. Fix shape: add staging + vault layers for RSEG (sat_invoice_item) and zmm_approval_log (stg_zmm__approval_log + sat_zmm_approval), then refactor the mart to source from vault. Allowlisted in scripts/check_rule3_layer_violations.py until those vault layers exist; remove the allowlist entry once refactored. Also: this mart was generated by piece_8_5_create_s2t_from_bar (per schema.yml meta), so the Create S2T flow itself is producing RULE 3 violations — separate downstream issue, file independently if it recurs after vault layers are added.
resolution: Built the missing vault layers and refactored the mart to source from vault. New models added: stg_sap__zmm_approval_log (Layer 1 staging for the ZMM_APPROVAL_LOG Z-table, with hash keys for hk_zmm_approval / hk_purchase_order / hk_invoice and hashdiff_zmm_approval); hub_zmm_approval (DV hub on APPROVAL_ID, 500 rows); sat_zmm_approval (DV sat with approval_status / approval_date / EBELN / EBELP / etc., 500 rows); sat_invoice_item (DV sat on hub_invoice carrying RSEG BUZEI line items, 2157 rows). EKBE BWART='101' rows substituted with sat_gr_item.movement_type='101' — verified data-equivalent: 2157 rows in both, 656072 total quantity, identical (EBELN, EBELP) groupings. Refactored fact_po_invoice_match_variance to source from sat_po_header + sat_po_item + link_po_material + link_po_vendor (PO side), sat_gr_item (GR), sat_invoice_item (invoice), sat_zmm_approval (exceptions). Strict parity verified: 184->184 rows, 2200 total_po_lines / 90.48% avg matched_pct / 135 exceptions / 105 approved / 30 open / 8 vendors / 26 months — all identical to baseline. dbt test 3/3 PASS. Allowlist entry removed from scripts/check_rule3_layer_violations.py — checker now exits 0 with no violations across the entire downstream layer. Sources.yml updated to register raw_sap.zmm_approval_log.

title: dim_equipment uniqueness fails - link_equipment_material fan-out (1 device -> multiple materials)
description: link_equipment_material has 75236 rows for 45000 distinct equipment; 30236 devices (~67%) link to >1 material (e.g. CPE-00000330, an ONT, links to both CPE-ONT-003 and CPE-RTR-001). This fans dim_equipment out to 75236 rows, breaking unique_dim_equipment_equipment_number and unique_dim_equipment_hk_equipment (30236 dups each). Root cause is link_equipment_material grain: equipment receives multiple GR/material-document associations and no authoritative-material selection is applied. PRE-EXISTING and orthogonal to the SD/FI build - entire lineage (dim_equipment.sql @717e777 2026-04-14, link_equipment_material) unchanged by the 2026-06-25 build; surfaced during the 2026-06-26 build-health verification (dbt build PASS=394 ERROR=5). Likely fix: link picks authoritative material per equipment, or dim_equipment dedups to current material.

title: All LLM pipeline stages pinned to retired model claude-sonnet-4-20250514 (API 404) - blocks scope derivation, EDA, S2T
description: ~12 files hardcode MODEL/_MODEL = "claude-sonnet-4-20250514", which Anthropic retired - the messages API returns 404 not_found_error as of 2026-06-26, so every LLM stage is dead in the real Streamlit app. Affected: app/claude_api.py:24, scripts/_scope_derivation.py:67, _context_assembler.py:95, classify_source_columns.py:64, compile_semantic_model.py:78, run_catalog_backfill.py:62, run_code_tables_analysis.py:73, run_completeness_analysis.py:53, run_dimensions_analysis.py:72, run_magnitude_analysis.py:63, run_term_eda.py:53, sync_s2t_plain_from_dbt.py:44. run_term_injection.py:79 also pins claude-sonnet-4-5 (verify). Current Sonnet claude-sonnet-4-6 returns 200. Fix: global swap to claude-sonnet-4-6 and ideally centralize the model id in one config module. Surfaced while running Stage A propose for BG030; that run used a local in-process override to claude-sonnet-4-6.
resolution: Swapped all 12 MODEL/_MODEL pins claude-sonnet-4-20250514 -> claude-sonnet-4-6 (app/claude_api.py, _scope_derivation.py, _context_assembler.py, classify_source_columns.py, compile_semantic_model.py, run_catalog_backfill.py, run_{code_tables,completeness,dimensions,magnitude}_analysis.py, run_term_eda.py, sync_s2t_plain_from_dbt.py). Left run_term_injection.py pricing-dict historical key intact; its claude-sonnet-4-5 MODEL_SONNET still returns 200 (not retired). claude-sonnet-4-6 verified 200. Follow-up (deferred): centralize model id in one config module instead of 12 literals.

title: Scope-derivation validator rejected valid resolves_in 'source_diagnostic_required' (prompt/validator enum drift)
description: scope_derivation_prompt.md:100 defines source_diagnostic_required as a valid warning-severity resolves_in (Stage F, analyst may still confirm) and Source Diagnostic is a real Data Catalog feature, but _scope_derivation.py:755 valid_resolves_in omitted it. Any proposal whose blockers routed to source_diagnostic_required got validation_issues and could not be confirmed. Surfaced confirming BG030 Stage A scope.
resolution: Added 'source_diagnostic_required' to valid_resolves_in at _scope_derivation.py:755.

title: Stage A scope-history iter_num collides (propose+revise both iter 1) -> confirm can select wrong iteration
description: append_iteration_to_history writes only business_glossary.csv, but _propose_or_revise derives iter_num from the DB term row (scope_derivation_history_json), which stays {} until a dbt seed runs. So a revise after a propose (no reseed between) reads empty history and is numbered iter_num=1 again, colliding with the propose. confirm_scope(iter_num=1) then matches the first iter_num=1 (the propose), not the revise. Observed confirming BG030: confirmed the 9-table propose rather than the 7-table revise. CSV-vs-DB read split, same family as #53. Fix options: derive iter_num from CSV history in _propose_or_revise, or reseed/refresh DB history after each append. Confirm should also reject duplicate iter_num.

title: run_term_eda.py crashes (KeyError missing_dar_tables) when Term EDA prerequisites not met
description: run_term_eda.py:569 referenced prereq['missing_dar_tables'] but check_term_eda_prereq (_term_eda_prereq.py) returns missing_analyzers_per_table / missing_grain_pairs - no missing_dar_tables key. Any prereq failure (e.g. scope tables lacking domain EDA) crashed with KeyError instead of returning the clean 'prerequisites not met' report. Surfaced running Stage C-prime for BG030 with only VBRP profiled.
resolution: Derive missing_dar_tables from missing_analyzers_per_table.keys(); also surface missing_grain_pairs in the result.

title: Stage A scope-derivation _MAX_TOKENS=6000 truncates rich/wide-scope terms into invalid JSON
description: _scope_derivation.py _MAX_TOKENS=6000 was sized for ~9-table terms; the 14-table customer-centric BG030 produced a longer JSON proposal that truncated mid-string -> JSONDecodeError. Raised to 16000.
resolution: _MAX_TOKENS 6000 -> 16000 in scripts/_scope_derivation.py.

title: Synthetic data: BWART=201 deployment movements lack serial-number linkage (only 101/GR linked)
description: All SERI (45000) and SER03 (2155) serial records point to BWART=101 goods-receipt movements; the 27000 BWART=201 deployment movements have ZERO serial/equipment linkage. So per-device deployment date cannot be derived from the 201 movement (forced the first-bill proxy for null EQUI.INBDT in BG030, decision #119). Production fix: extend the sample-data generator to emit serial records for 201 goods-issue movements. Reopens with real SAP data where deployment movements are serial-linked.

title: Stage D (create_s2t) generates marts that ref() staging directly, violating RULE 3 (vault-only) - blocks commit gate
description: The generated fact_contribution_margin_by_segment refs 10 stg_sap__* models directly (vbrk,vbrp,vbak,vbap,ekko,ekpo,equi,seri,mkpf,mseg). anti_patterns.md RULE 3 requires mart/obt/knowledge models to ref() vault models only. end_of_task.py check_rule3_layer_violations.py flags this as BLOCKING. Root cause: the create_s2t context bundle surfaces staging/raw scope (dbt_semantic_model_consumed = stg_sap__*) and the generation prompt does not require targeting the vault layer, so the LLM builds the mart on staging. The model is otherwise correct (builds, grain clean, revenue reconciles). Resolution options: (a) refactor the fact onto vault sats/links/hubs (needs FKSTO/NETPR/INBDT exposed in sats - verify), or (b) add a vault-layering directive to the create_s2t prompt and regenerate.
resolution: Refactored fact_contribution_margin_by_segment onto vault-only refs (sat_billing_document, link_billing_sales_order, link_sales_order_equipment, sat_sales_order_item, sat_equipment_general w/ SCD2 current-version dedup, link_equipment_gr->link_gr_po->link_po_material->sat_po_item). Revenue penny-exact (EUR9,494,584), grain clean, RULE 3 OK. Prevention: added DIRECTIVE 3f-LAYERING to the create_s2t prompt (claude_api.py) requiring vault-only refs for mart/obt/knowledge models.

title: SD/FI vault models lack dbt_semantic_model/column-lineage in create_s2t bundle - generator refs vault (post-#129) but guesses SAP column names + hallucinates models
description: After #129's RULE 3 directive, create_s2t correctly refs VAULT models for marts/obt (verified on test term BG031: vault-only refs, 0 staging). But the bundle surfaces vault model NAMES without their business COLUMN names, so the LLM uses staging/SAP column names (FKDAT, FKSTO, NETWR, VBELN) that do not exist in the vault sats (which use billing_date, cancelled_flag, revenue_amount, payer_id) and hallucinated a non-existent sat (sat_billing_item). Result: vault-LAYERED but UNBUILDABLE SQL. Fix: add dbt_semantic_model (Layer B) / dbt_column_lineage coverage for the SD/FI vault models so the bundle exposes their real columns; then the #129 directive is sufficient for auto-buildable vault marts. (Manual BG030 vault refactor built fine because the human knew the real column names.) Fix-path = grounding + verification, not trust: (1) surface vault column schemas in the bundle (dbt_semantic_model exposed_columns_json / dbt_column_lineage); (2) add a deterministic column-existence pre-flight that checks every referenced column against the target vault model actual columns (information_schema/dbt catalog) and rejects/repairs on mismatch; (3) dbt build is the hard backstop - it cannot materialize a model referencing a non-existent column/model. LLM hallucination is CAUGHT, not prevented by prompting.
resolution: Implemented app/_vault_grounding.py: (1) vault_schema_block() injects real vault column schemas into the create_s2t prompt (grounding); (2) verify_vault_columns() pre-flight flags <alias>.<col> refs to vault models where the column is not real; (3) verify_no_staging_refs() hard-enforces RULE 3 (marts/obt vault-only). Wired into the create_s2t F.4 retry loop (MAX 2->3): staging-ref and bad-column violations now reject + retry with the real columns. Verified end-to-end on fresh term BG031: attempt 1 reached staging -> rejected -> regenerated vault-only -> column audit clean -> dbt run OK -> revenue penny-exact EUR9,494,584. dbt build + repair_dbt_model_sql remain the deploy-time hard backstop.

title: grain_relationship pre-filter requires shared numeric column names (SAP-shaped)
description: The analyzer's pair pre-filter needs a shared numeric column NAME between two tables (SAP header/detail convention). On Olist, where numeric column names never repeat across tables, it emitted 0 pairs — the sum-match heuristic produces no evidence on sources with distinct naming. join_cardinality covers the gap partially.
resolution: Pre-filter no longer requires a shared numeric column name; pairs qualify on a shared join key and analyze_pair emits an explicit canonical skipped DAR when sum-match does not apply. On Olist: 0 pairs/total silence -> 10 pairs with explicit 'no shared numeric columns' records.

title: FK/join discovery misses semantically-joinable columns with different names
description: Olist geolocation.geolocation_zip_code_prefix joins customers.customer_zip_code_prefix / sellers.seller_zip_code_prefix — the dataset's most dangerous fanout (up to 1,146 rows per key) — but shared-name matching and schema_discovery FK detection both miss it. No cardinality evidence exists for the join, so F.3 passes it through unexamined. Needs fuzzy/suffix name matching or value-overlap probing.
resolution: join_cardinality gained candidate Source D: suffix-token name matching (customer_zip_code_prefix <-> geolocation_zip_code_prefix via shared suffix zip_code_prefix), validated empirically like all candidates. The geolocation joins are now discovered and classified: customers x geolocation avg 65.4x, sellers x geolocation avg 128.3x, both catastrophic_fanout, so F.3's lazy lookup now gates them. Inert on sources without underscore-composed names.

title: schema_discovery relationship shapes use distinct-key ratios, not row multiplicity
description: orders<->order_payments was shaped one_to_one (distinct order_id counts match) although 2,961 orders have multiple payment rows (avg 1.03, max 3). join_cardinality's per-direction measurements are the authoritative evidence; the shape field is cosmetic-but-misleading. Consider deriving shapes from the direction_fanout data.
resolution: Shape classification now uses per-side key duplication (dup >= 2 and dup > 0.1% of rows) instead of a 1.2 count-ratio threshold. orders<->order_payments correctly classifies header_detail (1:N); customers<->orders stays one_to_one. Verified on Olist and on the SAP unit-test fixtures.

title: BG033 semantic validator warnings pending analyst review
description: fact_repeat_customer_rate passed semantic validation (match=true) with two warnings: (1) the first-order baseline excludes canceled/unavailable orders, so a person whose first-ever order was canceled is misclassified as first-time on their next order; (2) the mart also excludes status='unavailable' from the denominator, which the term definition does not specify (3.0% vs 3.017% overall). Both need an analyst ruling on the term contract.
resolution: Analyst ruling applied via repair_semantic_mismatch: numerator/denominator exclude only order_status='canceled' (matching the GMV mart and the term contract), and the per-person first-order baseline is computed across ALL orders regardless of status ('placed at least one earlier order' includes canceled orders). Deployed value moved 3.0% -> 3.044%, independently reconciled. External reviewer also flagged the cross-mart filter drift; both marts now apply canceled-only.

title: Deploy step d.5 semantic gate reads OBT metadata before the view materializes
description: During BG032's UI deploy, step d.5 logged 'metadata read failed: Catalog Error: Table obt_ecommerce_on_time_delivery does not exist' for the OBT while validating it - the gate ran a beat before dbt created the view; it degraded gracefully (warn) and dbt test subsequently passed. Sequencing: the semantic gate should run after the model it validates is materialized, or retry once after step e. Non-fatal; deployed mart independently reconciled (91.888%).

title: F.3 validator fails open on internal errors
description: When the F.3 cardinality validator raises, create_s2t logs '[WARN] F.3 validator raised: ...; skipping enforcement' and treats validation as passed. Review round 3: the gate that guards catastrophic joins fails open, so a validator bug disables the protection silently. Consider fail-closed with a single retry, or requiring explicit analyst acknowledgement in the result payload.
resolution: F.3 now fails closed: the validator gets one retry for transient errors, and a persistent failure returns a structured refusal (f3_validator_unavailable) instead of silently skipping enforcement. Pinned by tests (persistent failure refuses; single transient failure retries and proceeds).

title: Join-key discovery: value-overlap probing (completes #132)
description: The #132 fix added suffix-token name matching, which found the zip-prefix joins but cannot see semantically joinable keys with unrelated names (cust_id vs customer_ref). The designed completion is value-overlap probing: for type-compatible column pairs, sample values and measure overlap; candidates above threshold enter the same empirical measurement pipeline. Bounded cost via sampling and per-pair caps.
resolution: Source E (value-overlap probing) added to join-candidate enumeration: type-compatible, key-shaped column pairs are probed by sampling distinct values (deterministic hash ordering, 200-value sample, 50% threshold, 20 probes per pair); hits enter the standard empirical measurement. Key-shaped means strings above a small cardinality floor and integers at surrogate-key cardinality; floats and small-int sequences are excluded after the first live sweep showed payment amounts 'joining' product weights by value-range coincidence. Verified: finds cust_id<->customer_ref (unit test) and discovered the cross-named city/state join hazards on Olist (customer_state x seller_state avg 4288x, recorded catastrophic).

title: Vault hash-key recipe divergence across generation sessions
description: BG035's generated satellite computed hk_olist_order as MD5(UPPER(TRIM(order_id))) while the existing hub used dbt_utils.generate_surrogate_key. Same natural key, different hashes: every join missed and the deployed mart was EMPTY while dbt run/test passed green. Found by the analyst-verification reconciliation step (independent SQL said 64.1576%; mart said nothing).
resolution: Three-part fix: (1) the three BG035 vault models repaired to generate_surrogate_key and full-refreshed - mart now reconciles exactly at 64.1576% (61,898/96,478); (2) hash-key convention added as a hard rule in the vault grounding block so generation cannot diverge again; (3) deterministic zero-row gate added as deploy step d.4 - deployed models with zero rows now hard-fail before semantic validation.

title: UI staging references hardcoded stg_sap__ prefix
description: Term Detail data profiling (and three sibling code paths in Business_Glossary.py plus the shared SQL rewriter in _data_analysis_shared.py) built staging names as stg_sap__<table> regardless of active source, producing 'Could not profile main_staging.stg_sap__orders' errors on Olist terms. Found by the analyst during the BG035 UI walk.
resolution: Source-aware staging_prefix()/source_schema() helpers added to _data_analysis_shared.py (env-config driven); all five hardcode sites now use them.

title: Parquet ghosts: exporter never pruned dropped tables
description: export_parquet.py only ever COPYs; tables dropped from the DuckDB (decommissioned seeds from 2026-04, archived marts, a __dbt_backup artifact) survived forever as parquet files, and the app's parquet-backed connection kept serving them. Analyst noticed the Seeds Catalog diagnostic: 37 seeds in main_seeds vs 31 CSVs in dbt/seeds.
resolution: Exporter now prunes any parquet file without a corresponding live DB table after each export (10 ghosts removed on first run: 6 decommissioned seeds, 2 archived marts, 1 archived OBT view, 1 dbt backup artifact). main_seeds parquet count now equals the CSV count.

title: Olist CI red for 5 runs: cross-source dim_date dependency + nobody watched the guard
description: BG032's and BG035's generated OBT views LEFT JOIN dim_date (the conformed calendar mart) - a legitimate Kimball pattern, but the Olist CI workflow built only models/olist, so the clean-clone build lacked dim_date and failed from c8ec981 onward. Five consecutive red runs went unnoticed until the analyst asked about GitHub failure emails. Bonus finding: dim_date's spine started at 2023, so the calendar joins produced NULLs against Olist's 2016-2018 data even locally.
resolution: CI builds ancestors (dbt build --select +models/olist); dim_date spine widened to 2016-01-01 (conformed across source eras); the workflow now also asserts the two newer published numbers (on-time 91.888, cross-state 61898/96478) so all four Olist results are rot-guarded. Process lesson: check the workflow result after every push that touches olist models.
